      "source": [
        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "The previous code failed because the `generate_run_manifest_fingerprint` function in `provenance_tools.py` attempted to serialize the simulation parameters dictionary, which contains complex numbers (`reset_value`, `source`), to JSON. JSON does not have a native complex number type, leading to a `TypeError: Object of type complex is not JSON serializable`. Although the `provenance_tools` module already includes logic to handle NumPy generic types, it needs to be extended to handle standard Python `complex` types explicitly before JSON serialization. I will modify the `generate_run_manifest_fingerprint` function in `provenance_tools.py` to convert complex numbers to a serializable format (e.g., a string or a dictionary representation) before passing the parameters to `json.dumps`.\n",
        "\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "HoWwmEouuh0k",
        "outputId": "dec2359e-e143-4e72-b47a-2968ecdaaf43"
      },
      "source": [
        "%%writefile provenance_tools.py\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "This module provides a toolkit for ensuring scientific reproducibility in\n",
        "simulation runs, extracted from the IRER project's adaptive hunt scripts.\n",
        "\n",
        "It includes functions for:\n",
        "1.  File Fingerprinting (SHA256)\n",
        "2.  Run Manifest Generation (Hashing all outputs and params)\n",
        "3.  Hash-Based Feedback Seed Generation (for deterministic adaptive loops)\n",
        "4.  CSV Ledger Logging\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "import os\n",
        "import json\n",
        "import hashlib\n",
        "import pathlib\n",
        "from typing import Dict, Any\n",
        "\n",
        "def save_fingerprint(file_path: str, verbose: bool = False):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Calculates the SHA256 hash of a single file and saves it to a\n",
        "    corresponding .sha256sum file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    This is a core component for verifying data integrity.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        path_obj = pathlib.Path(file_path)\n",
        "        if path_obj.is_file():\n",
        "            file_hash = hashlib.sha256()\n",
        "            with open(path_obj, 'rb') as f:\n",
        "                # Read in chunks for large files\n",
        "                while chunk := f.read(8192):\n",
        "                    file_hash.update(chunk)\n",
        "\n",
        "            fingerprint = file_hash.hexdigest()\n",
        "            sha256_file_path = path_obj.with_suffix(path_obj.suffix + \".sha256sum\")\n",
        "\n",
        "            with open(sha256_file_path, 'w', newline='\\n') as f_sha:\n",
        "                f_sha.write(fingerprint + f\" *{path_obj.name}\\n\")\n",
        "\n",
        "            if verbose:\n",
        "                print(f\"  SHA256: {path_obj.name} -> {sha256_file_path.name}\")\n",
        "        elif verbose:\n",
        "            print(f\"  Warning (fingerprint): File not found: {file_path}\")\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"  Error creating fingerprint for {file_path}: {e}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def generate_run_manifest_fingerprint(run_output_dir: str,\n",
        "                                      params_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n",
        "                                      verbose: bool = False) -> str:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Generates a master manifest file for a simulation run.\n",
        "\n",
        "    This file includes:\n",
        "    - The simulation parameters used.\n",
        "    - SHA256 hashes of all key (non-binary) output files.\n",
        "    - An overall manifest hash for the entire run's provenance.\n",
        "\n",
        "    This is the primary function for run logging and reproducibility.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    overall_manifest_hash_value = \"GENERATION_FAILED\"\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        run_dir_path = pathlib.Path(run_output_dir)\n",
        "        if not run_dir_path.is_dir():\n",
        "            if verbose: print(f\"  Warning (manifest): Directory not found: {run_output_dir}\");\n",
        "            return overall_manifest_hash_value\n",
        "\n",
        "        file_manifest_details = []\n",
        "        # Recursively find all files in the run directory\n",
        "        for p in sorted(run_dir_path.rglob(\"*\")):\n",
        "            # Exclude large binaries (like .npy) and other manifests\n",
        "            if p.is_file() and not p.name.endswith((\".sha256sum\", \".npz\", \".npy\")) and p.name != \"run_manifest.sha256sum\":\n",
        "                try:\n",
        "                    file_hash = hashlib.sha256()\n",
        "                    with open(p, 'rb') as f_p:\n",
        "                        while chunk := f_p.read(8192):\n",
        "                            file_hash.update(chunk)\n",
        "                    relative_path_str = str(p.relative_to(run_dir_path)).replace(\"\\\\\", \"/\")\n",
        "                    file_manifest_details.append(f\"{file_hash.hexdigest()} *{relative_path_str}\")\n",
        "                except Exception as e_file:\n",
        "                    if verbose: print(f\"  Error hashing file {p} for manifest: {e_file}\")\n",
        "                    relative_path_str = str(p.relative_to(run_dir_path)).replace(\"\\\\\", \"/\")\n",
        "                    file_manifest_details.append(f\"ERROR_HASHING *{relative_path_str}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        manifest_string = \"\\n\".join(sorted(file_manifest_details))\n",
        "\n",
        "        # --- Serialize parameters for the manifest ---\n",
        "        # Ensure NumPy types and complex numbers are converted to standard Python types for JSON\n",
        "        serializable_params = {}\n",
        "        if params_dict:\n",
        "            for k, v in params_dict.items():\n",
        "                if isinstance(v, np.generic):\n",
        "                    serializable_params[k] = v.item()\n",
        "                elif isinstance(v, complex):\n",
        "                     # Convert complex to a string representation\n",
        "                     serializable_params[k] = str(v)\n",
        "                else:\n",
        "                    serializable_params[k] = v\n",
        "\n",
        "        provenance_content = \"--- Parameters ---\\n\"\n",
        "        provenance_content += json.dumps(serializable_params, sort_keys=True, indent=2) + \"\\n\"\n",
        "\n",
        "        # --- Combine all provenance info for a final hash ---\n",
        "        full_manifest_content_for_hashing = provenance_content + \"--- File Hashes (selected) ---\\n\" + manifest_string\n",
        "        overall_manifest_hash_value = hashlib.sha256(full_manifest_content_for_hashing.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()\n",
        "\n",
        "        # --- Write the manifest file ---\n",
        "        manifest_file_path = run_dir_path / \"run_manifest.sha256sum\"\n",
        "        with open(manifest_file_path, 'w', newline='\\n') as f_manifest:\n",
        "            f_manifest.write(f\"Overall Run Manifest Hash (SHA256 of below content sections): {overall_manifest_hash_value}\\n\\n\")\n",
        "            if params_dict:\n",
        "                f_manifest.write(\"--- Parameters ---\\n\")\n",
        "                json.dump(serializable_params, f_manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True)\n",
        "                f_manifest.write(\"\\n\\n\")\n",
        "            f_manifest.write(\"--- Individual File SHA256 Hashes (*relative_path_to_file, selected files) ---\\n\")\n",
        "            f_manifest.write(manifest_string + \"\\n\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        if verbose: print(f\"  Run manifest for '{run_output_dir}' saved to {manifest_file_path.name}\")\n",
        "        return overall_manifest_hash_value\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"  Error creating run manifest for {run_output_dir}: {e}\")\n",
        "        return overall_manifest_hash_value\n",
        "\n",
        "def compute_feedback_seed_adaptive(summary_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> int:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Computes the next seed for an adaptive loop based on the summary\n",
        "    of the previous run. This creates a deterministic, hash-based chain\n",
        "    for reproducible adaptive searches.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Extract key metrics that define the run's outcome\n",
        "    key_metrics = {\n",
        "        'total_collapses': summary_dict.get('total_collapses'),\n",
        "        'avg_rho_final': summary_dict.get('avg_rho_final'),\n",
        "        'max_rho_overall': summary_dict.get('max_rho_overall'),\n",
        "        'k_peak_value': summary_dict.get('k_peak_value'),\n",
        "        'k_peak_met_target': summary_dict.get('k_peak_met_target'),\n",
        "        'is_coherent': summary_dict.get('is_coherent'),\n",
        "    }\n",
        "\n",
        "    evo_round = summary_dict.get('simulation_parameters', {}).get('evolution_round', -1)\n",
        "    manifest_hash = summary_dict.get('overall_manifest_hash', \"\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    if not manifest_hash or manifest_hash == \"GENERATION_FAILED\":\n",
        "        print(\"Warning: Manifest hash not available for feedback seed. Seed will be less robustly deterministic.\")\n",
        "        # Fallback to hashing parameters if manifest failed\n",
        "        params_for_hash = summary_dict.get('simulation_parameters', {})\n",
        "        serializable_params = {k: (v.item() if isinstance(v, np.generic) else v) for k, v in params_for_hash.items()}\n",
        "        manifest_hash = hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(serializable_params, sort_keys=True).encode()).hexdigest()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # --- Serialize all metrics for hashing ---\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        serializable_key_metrics = {}\n",
        "        for k, v in key_metrics.items():\n",
        "            if isinstance(v, (np.bool_, bool)):\n",
        "                serializable_key_metrics[k] = bool(v)\n",
        "            elif isinstance(v, (np.integer, int)):\n",
        "                serializable_key_metrics[k] = int(v)\n",
        "            elif isinstance(v, (np.floating, float)):\n",
        "                serializable_key_metrics[k] = float(v) if np.isfinite(v) else None\n",
        "            elif v is None:\n",
        "                 serializable_key_metrics[k] = None\n",
        "            elif isinstance(v, complex): # Handle complex numbers for serialization\n",
        "                 serializable_key_metrics[k] = str(v)\n",
        "            else:\n",
        "                serializable_key_metrics[k] = str(v)\n",
        "\n",
        "        combined_data_str = (\n",
        "            json.dumps(serializable_key_metrics, sort_keys=True) +\n",
        "            f\"evo_round:{evo_round}\" +\n",
        "            str(manifest_hash)\n",
        "        )\n",
        "    except TypeError as e_json:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error serializing key_metrics for feedback seed: {e_json}\")\n",
        "        combined_data_str = str(key_metrics) + f\"evo_round:{evo_round}\" + str(manifest_hash)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # --- Generate the new seed ---\n",
        "    new_seed_hex = hashlib.sha256(combined_data_str.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()\n",
        "    # Use top 8 hex chars (32 bits) for the new seed\n",
        "    new_seed = int(new_seed_hex[:8], 16)\n",
        "    return new_seed\n",
        "\n",
        "def log_run_to_csv_ledger(ledger_path: str, summary_dict: Dict[str, Any]):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Appends a single run's summary and parameters to a CSV ledger file.\n",
        "    This function is designed to be called after each simulation run.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Flatten the nested parameter dictionary for clean CSV columns\n",
        "        ledger_entry = {}\n",
        "        sim_params = summary_dict.get('simulation_parameters', {})\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Add summary metrics\n",
        "        ledger_entry['evolution_round'] = sim_params.get('evolution_round')\n",
        "        ledger_entry['global_seed'] = sim_params.get('global_seed')\n",
        "        ledger_entry['run_name'] = sim_params.get('run_name_suffix')\n",
        "        ledger_entry['manifest_hash'] = summary_dict.get('overall_manifest_hash')\n",
        "        ledger_entry['total_collapses'] = summary_dict.get('total_collapses')\n",
        "        ledger_entry['avg_rho_final'] = summary_dict.get('avg_rho_final')\n",
        "        ledger_entry['max_rho_overall'] = summary_dict.get('max_rho_overall')\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Add analysis metrics if they exist\n",
        "        if 'k_peak_value' in summary_dict:\n",
        "            ledger_entry['k_peak_value'] = summary_dict.get('k_peak_value')\n",
        "            ledger_entry['k_peak_met_target'] = summary_dict.get('k_peak_met_target')\n",
        "            ledger_entry['is_coherent'] = summary_dict.get('is_coherent')\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Add all simulation parameters, prefixed with 'param_'\n",
        "        for p_key, p_val in sim_params.items():\n",
        "            # Convert complex numbers to string for CSV compatibility\n",
        "            if isinstance(p_val, complex):\n",
        "                ledger_entry[f\"param_{p_key}\"] = str(p_val)\n",
        "            elif isinstance(p_val, np.generic):\n",
        "                ledger_entry[f\"param_{p_key}\"] = p_val.item()\n",
        "            else:\n",
        "                ledger_entry[f\"param_{p_key}\"] = p_val\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Convert to DataFrame to handle CSV operations robustly\n",
        "        df_entry = pd.DataFrame([ledger_entry])\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Check if file exists to write header or append\n",
        "        file_exists = os.path.isfile(ledger_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "        df_entry.to_csv(ledger_path, mode='a', header=not file_exists, index=False)\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error updating CSV ledger at {ledger_path}: {e}\")"
      ],

      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "c6f8943d",
        "outputId": "93dcea7b-494b-4ac3-c5a3-def604070fdb"
      },
      "source": [
        "# The necessary imports are already in the cell.\n",
        "# import os\n",
        "# import tempfile\n",
        "# import analysis_modules as am\n",
        "# import provenance_tools as pt\n",
        "# import copy # Import copy for creating a mutable copy of sim_params\n",
        "# import time # Import time for measuring execution time\n",
        "\n",
        "# Define a directory for saving analysis outputs\n",
        "ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR = \"simulation_analysis_outputs\"\n",
        "LEDGER_FILE_PATH = \"simulation_ledger.csv\"\n",
        "\n",
        "# Create the directory if it doesn't exist\n",
        "if not os.path.exists(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR):\n",
        "    os.makedirs(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR)\n",
        "    print(f\"Created analysis output directory: {ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 1. Simulation Setup ---\n",
        "GRID_SIZE = 64\n",
        "DT = 0.01\n",
        "STEPS = 1000 # Increase steps to allow for analysis intervals\n",
        "ANALYSIS_INTERVAL = 100 # Run analysis every 100 steps\n",
        "\n",
        "# Define parameters based on the S-NCGL blueprints\n",
        "# These values are illustrative\n",
        "sim_params = {\n",
        "    'epsilon': 0.1,         # Linear growth\n",
        "    'D': 1.0,               # Diffusion coefficient (real part of ∇²)\n",
        "    'c1': 0.5,              # Linear dispersion (imaginary part of ∇²)\n",
        "    'b3': 1.0,               # Nonlinear saturation (real part of |A|²A)\n",
        "    'c3': -1.5,             # Nonlinear phase shift (imag. part of |A|²A)\n",
        "    'threshold': 1.2,       # Collapse threshold for rho = |A|²\n",
        "    'splash_fraction': 0.5, # Fraction of A to redistribute\n",
        "    'splash_kernel_sigma': 4.0, # Width of Gaussian splash\n",
        "    'reset_value': 0.0 + 0.0j,  # Value to reset collapsed cells to\n",
        "    'source': 0.0 + 0.0j    # Static source term (can be a 3D array)\n",
        "}\n",
        "\n",
        "# Add run-specific parameters for the ledger/manifest\n",
        "# Using a simple counter for run_id/evolution_round in this single script context\n",
        "run_id_counter = 0\n",
        "sim_params['evolution_round'] = run_id_counter\n",
        "sim_params['global_seed'] = 42 # Using the initial JAX PRNG key seed\n",
        "sim_params['run_name_suffix'] = f\"step_{STEPS}\" # Descriptive name\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 2. Pre-compute Kernels ---\n",
        "print(\"Pre-computing kernels...\")\n",
        "k_squared, K_fft = precompute_kernels(\n",
        "    GRID_SIZE,\n",
        "    sim_params['splash_kernel_sigma']\n",
        ")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 3. Initialize State ---\n",
        "print(\"Initializing complex field A...\")\n",
        "key = jax.random.PRNGKey(sim_params['global_seed'])\n",
        "key_real, key_imag = jax.random.split(key)\n",
        "\n",
        "shape = (GRID_SIZE, GRID_SIZE, GRID_SIZE)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Initialize with small random complex values\n",
        "A_field_initial = (\n",
        "    jax.random.normal(key_real, shape) * 0.1 +\n",
        "    1j * jax.random.normal(key_imag, shape) * 0.1\n",
        ")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 4. JIT Compilation (Warm-up) ---\n",
        "print(\"Compiling JAX function...\")\n",
        "start_compile = time.time()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Run the function once to compile it\n",
        "# Pass only the JAX-compatible parameters for compilation\n",
        "jit_params = {k: v for k, v in sim_params.items() if isinstance(v, (float, int, complex, jnp.ndarray))}\n",
        "\n",
        "A_field_compiled = s_ncgl_full_step(\n",
        "    A_field_initial,\n",
        "    jit_params, # Pass only JAX-compatible params\n",
        "    k_squared,\n",
        "    K_fft,\n",
        "    GRID_SIZE,\n",
        "    DT\n",
        ").block_until_ready()\n",
        "\n",
        "end_compile = time.time()\n",
        "print(f\"Compilation finished in {end_compile - start_compile:.4f} seconds.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 5. Run Simulation ---\n",
        "print(f\"Running simulation for {STEPS} steps with analysis every {ANALYSIS_INTERVAL} steps...\")\n",
        "A_field_current = A_field_compiled\n",
        "\n",
        "start_run = time.time()\n",
        "\n",
        "# We can run the loop in Python\n",
        "for i in range(STEPS):\n",
        "    # Pass only the JAX-compatible parameters to the jitted function\n",
        "    jit_params = {k: v for k, v in sim_params.items() if isinstance(v, (float, int, complex, jnp.ndarray))}\n",
        "    A_field_current = s_ncgl_full_step(\n",
        "        A_field_current,\n",
        "        jit_params, # Pass only JAX-compatible params\n",
        "        k_squared,\n",
        "        K_fft,\n",
        "        GRID_SIZE,\n",
        "        DT\n",
        "    )\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Run analysis at specified intervals and log provenance\n",
        "    if (i + 1) % ANALYSIS_INTERVAL == 0 or (i + 1) == STEPS:\n",
        "        current_step = i + 1\n",
        "        print(f\"\\n--- Running analysis after step {current_step} ---\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Create a subdirectory for this step's analysis outputs\n",
        "        step_output_dir = os.path.join(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR, f\"step_{current_step}\")\n",
        "        if not os.path.exists(step_output_dir):\n",
        "            os.makedirs(step_output_dir)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Save the current state to a temporary file\n",
        "        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=\".npy\", delete=False) as tmp_file:\n",
        "            temp_file_path = tmp_file.name\n",
        "            jnp.save(temp_file_path, A_field_current)\n",
        "\n",
        "        run_summary = {\n",
        "            'simulation_parameters': copy.deepcopy(sim_params), # Log ALL params for provenance\n",
        "            'analysis_step': current_step,\n",
        "            'total_collapses': 0, # Placeholder, needs to be calculated if needed\n",
        "            'avg_rho_final': float(jnp.mean(jnp.abs(A_field_current)**2)), # Mean rho at this step\n",
        "            'max_rho_overall': float(jnp.max(jnp.abs(A_field_current)**2)), # Max rho at this step\n",
        "            'k_peak_value': None,\n",
        "            'k_peak_met_target': None,\n",
        "            'is_coherent': None,\n",
        "            'j_info_mean_magnitude': None,\n",
        "            'overall_manifest_hash': \"GENERATION_FAILED\"\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            # Run Quantule Mapper analysis\n",
        "            print(\"Running Quantule Mapper...\")\n",
        "            quantule_results = am.run_quantule_mapper(temp_file_path)\n",
        "            run_summary['k_peak_value'] = float(quantule_results.get('dominant_peak_k', 0))\n",
        "            # Logic to determine if target met and coherence is needed here\n",
        "            # For now, leaving as None or placeholder\n",
        "            # run_summary['k_peak_met_target'] = ...\n",
        "            # run_summary['is_coherent'] = ...\n",
        "            run_summary['quantule_total_sse'] = float(quantule_results.get('total_sse', float('inf')))\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Save Quantule Mapper CSV outputs\n",
        "            if 'csv_files' in quantule_results:\n",
        "                for csv_name, df in quantule_results['csv_files'].items():\n",
        "                    output_filename = f\"{os.path.splitext(csv_name)[0]}.csv\" # Simplified filename for manifest\n",
        "                    output_path = os.path.join(step_output_dir, output_filename)\n",
        "                    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)\n",
        "                    # print(f\"  Saved {output_filename}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Run Informational Current analysis\n",
        "            print(\"Running Informational Current analysis...\")\n",
        "            j_info_field = am.compute_informational_current(temp_file_path)\n",
        "            j_info_mean_magnitude = jnp.mean(jnp.linalg.norm(j_info_field, axis=0))\n",
        "            run_summary['j_info_mean_magnitude'] = float(j_info_mean_magnitude)\n",
        "            # print(f\"  Mean magnitude of J_info: {j_info_mean_magnitude:.4f}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Save the full J_info_field (B-field analogue)\n",
        "            # Saving as .npy for potential later visualization, excluded from manifest hash\n",
        "            j_info_filename = \"j_info_field.npy\" # Simplified filename\n",
        "            j_info_path = os.path.join(step_output_dir, j_info_filename)\n",
        "            jnp.save(j_info_path, j_info_field)\n",
        "            # print(f\"  Saved {j_info_filename}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Generate Run Manifest and Fingerprint for this step's outputs\n",
        "            print(\"Generating run manifest...\")\n",
        "            # Pass a mutable copy of ALL sim_params for the manifest file content\n",
        "            manifest_params_for_file = copy.deepcopy(sim_params)\n",
        "            manifest_params_for_file['analysis_step'] = current_step # Add step info to manifest params\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Explicitly convert complex numbers to strings for JSON serialization within the manifest\n",
        "            serializable_manifest_params = {}\n",
        "            for k, v in manifest_params_for_file.items():\n",
        "                if isinstance(v, complex):\n",
        "                    serializable_manifest_params[k] = str(v)\n",
        "                elif isinstance(v, np.generic): # Also handle NumPy types explicitly if needed\n",
        "                    serializable_manifest_params[k] = v.item()\n",
        "                else:\n",
        "                    serializable_manifest_params[k] = v\n",
        "\n",
        "            manifest_hash = pt.generate_run_manifest_fingerprint(step_output_dir, serializable_manifest_params, verbose=True)\n",
        "            run_summary['overall_manifest_hash'] = manifest_hash\n",
        "            # print(f\"  Manifest hash: {manifest_hash}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Log run details to CSV ledger\n",
        "            print(\"Logging to CSV ledger...\")\n",
        "            # The run_summary already contains the full sim_params and analysis results\n",
        "            # log_run_to_csv_ledger also handles complex numbers, so pass run_summary directly\n",
        "            pt.log_run_to_csv_ledger(LEDGER_FILE_PATH, run_summary)\n",
        "            # print(\"  Ledger updated.\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"Analysis or Provenance logging failed at step {current_step}: {e}\")\n",
        "            run_summary['analysis_error'] = str(e)\n",
        "            # Still attempt to log partial summary to ledger if possible\n",
        "            pt.log_run_to_csv_ledger(LEDGER_FILE_PATH, run_summary)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        finally:\n",
        "            # Clean up the temporary file\n",
        "            if os.path.exists(temp_file_path):\n",
        "                os.remove(temp_file_path)\n",
        "                # print(f\"Removed temporary file: {temp_file_path}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "# Block to ensure all computation is finished before stopping timer\n",
        "A_field_current.block_until_ready()\n",
        "end_run = time.time()\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f\"\\nTotal simulation run time: {end_run - start_run:.4f} seconds.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 6. Final State Analysis and Provenance ---\n",
        "print(\"\\n--- Final State Summary ---\")\n",
        "final_rho = jnp.abs(A_field_current)**2\n",
        "print(f\"  Max Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.max(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",
        "print(f\"  Min Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.min(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",
        "print(f\"  Mean Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.mean(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# The final state was logged in the last iteration of the loop (i+1 == STEPS)\n",
        "# No need for a separate final state logging step for the ledger if the loop covers it.\n",
        "\n",
        "# Optional: Generate a final manifest for the entire ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR\n",
        "# This would be a manifest of manifests and other aggregated files.\n",
        "# print(\"\\nGenerating overall run manifest...\")\n",
        "# overall_run_manifest_hash = pt.generate_run_manifest_fingerprint(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR, copy.deepcopy(sim_params), verbose=True)\n",
        "# print(f\"Overall Run Manifest Hash: {overall_run_manifest_hash}\")"

        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "The provenance logging is still failing because the `generate_run_manifest_fingerprint` function, even after the previous modification, is still encountering `TypeError: Object of type complex is not JSON serializable`. The `sim_params` dictionary passed to this function contains complex numbers (`reset_value`, `source`), and although I attempted to handle this in the previous step within `provenance_tools.py`, the error persists. This suggests the fix in `provenance_tools.py` was not fully effective or correctly applied to all complex number instances, or there might be another part of the dictionary structure causing issues.\n",
        "\n",
        "I need to ensure that *all* values in the `sim_params` dictionary are properly converted to JSON-serializable types (like strings for complex numbers, standard floats/ints for numpy types) *before* the dictionary is passed to `json.dumps` within the `generate_run_manifest_fingerprint` function. The previous attempt modified `provenance_tools.py`, but it seems it wasn't sufficient.\n",
        "\n",
        "I will modify the simulation script cell (`xYpafu6ro3Om`) again. Inside the loop, just before calling `pt.generate_run_manifest_fingerprint`, I will create a new dictionary by iterating through `manifest_params_for_file` and explicitly converting any complex values to strings. This ensures the dictionary passed to `json.dumps` is fully serializable.\n",
        "\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "XaPQP_WEu2-p",
        "outputId": "f7f29d5c-274a-4d48-a9a5-d1c264e56fa9"
      },
      "source": [
        "# The necessary imports are already in the cell.\n",
        "# import os\n",
        "# import tempfile\n",
        "# import analysis_modules as am\n",
        "# import provenance_tools as pt\n",
        "# import copy # Import copy for creating a mutable copy of sim_params\n",
        "# import time # Import time for measuring execution time\n",
        "\n",
        "# Define a directory for saving analysis outputs\n",
        "ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR = \"simulation_analysis_outputs\"\n",
        "LEDGER_FILE_PATH = \"simulation_ledger.csv\"\n",
        "\n",
        "# Create the directory if it doesn't exist\n",
        "if not os.path.exists(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR):\n",
        "    os.makedirs(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR)\n",
        "    print(f\"Created analysis output directory: {ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 1. Simulation Setup ---\n",
        "GRID_SIZE = 64\n",
        "DT = 0.01\n",
        "STEPS = 1000 # Increase steps to allow for analysis intervals\n",
        "ANALYSIS_INTERVAL = 100 # Run analysis every 100 steps\n",
        "\n",
        "# Define parameters based on the S-NCGL blueprints\n",
        "# These values are illustrative\n",
        "sim_params = {\n",
        "    'epsilon': 0.1,         # Linear growth\n",
        "    'D': 1.0,               # Diffusion coefficient (real part of ∇²)\n",
        "    'c1': 0.5,              # Linear dispersion (imaginary part of ∇²)\n",
        "    'b3': 1.0,              # Nonlinear saturation (real part of |A|²A)\n",
        "    'c3': -1.5,             # Nonlinear phase shift (imag. part of |A|²A)\n",
        "    'threshold': 1.2,       # Collapse threshold for rho = |A|²\n",
        "    'splash_fraction': 0.5, # Fraction of A to redistribute\n",
        "    'splash_kernel_sigma': 4.0, # Width of Gaussian splash\n",
        "    'reset_value': 0.0 + 0.0j,  # Value to reset collapsed cells to\n",
        "    'source': 0.0 + 0.0j    # Static source term (can be a 3D array)\n",
        "}\n",
        "\n",
        "# Add run-specific parameters for the ledger/manifest\n",
        "# Using a simple counter for run_id/evolution_round in this single script context\n",
        "run_id_counter = 0\n",
        "sim_params['evolution_round'] = run_id_counter\n",
        "sim_params['global_seed'] = 42 # Using the initial JAX PRNG key seed\n",
        "sim_params['run_name_suffix'] = f\"step_{STEPS}\" # Descriptive name\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 2. Pre-compute Kernels ---\n",
        "print(\"Pre-computing kernels...\")\n",
        "k_squared, K_fft = precompute_kernels(\n",
        "    GRID_SIZE,\n",
        "    sim_params['splash_kernel_sigma']\n",
        ")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 3. Initialize State ---\n",
        "print(\"Initializing complex field A...\")\n",
        "key = jax.random.PRNGKey(sim_params['global_seed'])\n",
        "key_real, key_imag = jax.random.split(key)\n",
        "\n",
        "shape = (GRID_SIZE, GRID_SIZE, GRID_SIZE)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Initialize with small random complex values\n",
        "A_field_initial = (\n",
        "    jax.random.normal(key_real, shape) * 0.1 +\n",
        "    1j * jax.random.normal(key_imag, shape) * 0.1\n",
        ")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 4. JIT Compilation (Warm-up) ---\n",
        "print(\"Compiling JAX function...\")\n",
        "start_compile = time.time()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Run the function once to compile it\n",
        "# Pass only the JAX-compatible parameters for compilation\n",
        "# Ensure complex values are still passed for the simulation step itself\n",
        "jit_params = {k: v for k, v in sim_params.items() if isinstance(v, (float, int, complex, jnp.ndarray))}\n",
        "\n",
        "A_field_compiled = s_ncgl_full_step(\n",
        "    A_field_initial,\n",
        "    jit_params, # Pass only JAX-compatible params\n",
        "    k_squared,\n",
        "    K_fft,\n",
        "    GRID_SIZE,\n",
        "    DT\n",
        ").block_until_ready()\n",
        "\n",
        "end_compile = time.time()\n",
        "print(f\"Compilation finished in {end_compile - start_compile:.4f} seconds.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 5. Run Simulation ---\n",
        "print(f\"Running simulation for {STEPS} steps with analysis every {ANALYSIS_INTERVAL} steps...\")\n",
        "A_field_current = A_field_compiled\n",
        "\n",
        "start_run = time.time()\n",
        "\n",
        "# We can run the loop in Python\n",
        "for i in range(STEPS):\n",
        "    # Pass only the JAX-compatible parameters to the jitted function\n",
        "    # Ensure complex values are still passed for the simulation step itself\n",
        "    jit_params = {k: v for k, v in sim_params.items() if isinstance(v, (float, int, complex, jnp.ndarray))}\n",
        "    A_field_current = s_ncgl_full_step(\n",
        "        A_field_current,\n",
        "        jit_params, # Pass only JAX-compatible params\n",
        "        k_squared,\n",
        "        K_fft,\n",
        "        GRID_SIZE,\n",
        "        DT\n",
        "    )\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Run analysis at specified intervals and log provenance\n",
        "    if (i + 1) % ANALYSIS_INTERVAL == 0 or (i + 1) == STEPS:\n",
        "        current_step = i + 1\n",
        "        print(f\"\\n--- Running analysis after step {current_step} ---\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Create a subdirectory for this step's analysis outputs\n",
        "        step_output_dir = os.path.join(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR, f\"step_{current_step}\")\n",
        "        if not os.path.exists(step_output_dir):\n",
        "            os.makedirs(step_output_dir)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Save the current state to a temporary file\n",
        "        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=\".npy\", delete=False) as tmp_file:\n",
        "            temp_file_path = tmp_file.name\n",
        "            jnp.save(temp_file_path, A_field_current)\n",
        "\n",
        "        run_summary = {\n",
        "            'simulation_parameters': copy.deepcopy(sim_params), # Log ALL params for provenance\n",
        "            'analysis_step': current_step,\n",
        "            'total_collapses': 0, # Placeholder, needs to be calculated if needed\n",
        "            'avg_rho_final': float(jnp.mean(jnp.abs(A_field_current)**2)), # Mean rho at this step\n",
        "            'max_rho_overall': float(jnp.max(jnp.abs(A_field_current)**2)), # Max rho at this step\n",
        "            'k_peak_value': None,\n",
        "            'k_peak_met_target': None,\n",
        "            'is_coherent': None,\n",
        "            'j_info_mean_magnitude': None,\n",
        "            'overall_manifest_hash': \"GENERATION_FAILED\"\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            # Run Quantule Mapper analysis\n",
        "            print(\"Running Quantule Mapper...\")\n",
        "            quantule_results = am.run_quantule_mapper(temp_file_path)\n",
        "            run_summary['k_peak_value'] = float(quantule_results.get('dominant_peak_k', 0))\n",
        "            # Logic to determine if target met and coherence is needed here\n",
        "            # For now, leaving as None or placeholder\n",
        "            # run_summary['k_peak_met_target'] = ...\n",
        "            # run_summary['is_coherent'] = ...\n",
        "            run_summary['quantule_total_sse'] = float(quantule_results.get('total_sse', float('inf')))\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Save Quantule Mapper CSV outputs\n",
        "            if 'csv_files' in quantule_results:\n",
        "                for csv_name, df in quantule_results['csv_files'].items():\n",
        "                    output_filename = f\"{os.path.splitext(csv_name)[0]}.csv\" # Simplified filename for manifest\n",
        "                    output_path = os.path.join(step_output_dir, output_filename)\n",
        "                    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)\n",
        "                    # print(f\"  Saved {output_filename}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Run Informational Current analysis\n",
        "            print(\"Running Informational Current analysis...\")\n",
        "            j_info_field = am.compute_informational_current(temp_file_path)\n",
        "            j_info_mean_magnitude = jnp.mean(jnp.linalg.norm(j_info_field, axis=0))\n",
        "            run_summary['j_info_mean_magnitude'] = float(j_info_mean_magnitude)\n",
        "            # print(f\"  Mean magnitude of J_info: {j_info_mean_magnitude:.4f}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Save the full J_info_field (B-field analogue)\n",
        "            # Saving as .npy for potential later visualization, excluded from manifest hash\n",
        "            j_info_filename = \"j_info_field.npy\" # Simplified filename\n",
        "            j_info_path = os.path.join(step_output_dir, j_info_filename)\n",
        "            jnp.save(j_info_path, j_info_field)\n",
        "            # print(f\"  Saved {j_info_filename}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Generate Run Manifest and Fingerprint for this step's outputs\n",
        "            print(\"Generating run manifest...\")\n",
        "            # Pass a mutable copy of ALL sim_params for the manifest file content\n",
        "            manifest_params_for_file = copy.deepcopy(sim_params)\n",
        "            manifest_params_for_file['analysis_step'] = current_step # Add step info to manifest params\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Explicitly convert complex numbers to strings for JSON serialization within the manifest\n",
        "            serializable_manifest_params = {}\n",
        "            for k, v in manifest_params_for_file.items():\n",
        "                if isinstance(v, complex):\n",
        "                    serializable_manifest_params[k] = str(v)\n",
        "                elif isinstance(v, np.generic): # Also handle NumPy types explicitly if needed\n",
        "                    serializable_manifest_params[k] = v.item()\n",
        "                else:\n",
        "                    serializable_manifest_params[k] = v\n",
        "\n",
        "            manifest_hash = pt.generate_run_manifest_fingerprint(step_output_dir, serializable_manifest_params, verbose=True)\n",
        "            run_summary['overall_manifest_hash'] = manifest_hash\n",
        "            # print(f\"  Manifest hash: {manifest_hash}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Log run details to CSV ledger\n",
        "            print(\"Logging to CSV ledger...\")\n",
        "            # The run_summary already contains the full sim_params and analysis results\n",
        "            # log_run_to_csv_ledger also handles complex numbers, so pass run_summary directly\n",
        "            pt.log_run_to_csv_ledger(LEDGER_FILE_PATH, run_summary)\n",
        "            # print(\"  Ledger updated.\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"Analysis or Provenance logging failed at step {current_step}: {e}\")\n",
        "            run_summary['analysis_error'] = str(e)\n",
        "            # Still attempt to log partial summary to ledger if possible\n",
        "            pt.log_run_to_csv_ledger(LEDGER_FILE_PATH, run_summary)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        finally:\n",
        "            # Clean up the temporary file\n",
        "            if os.path.exists(temp_file_path):\n",
        "                os.remove(temp_file_path)\n",
        "                # print(f\"Removed temporary file: {temp_file_path}\") # Removed redundant print\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "# Block to ensure all computation is finished before stopping timer\n",
        "A_field_current.block_until_ready()\n",
        "end_run = time.time()\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f\"\\nTotal simulation run time: {end_run - start_run:.4f} seconds.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- 6. Final State Analysis and Provenance ---\n",
        "print(\"\\n--- Final State Summary ---\")\n",
        "final_rho = jnp.abs(A_field_current)**2\n",
        "print(f\"  Max Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.max(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",
        "print(f\"  Min Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.min(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",
        "print(f\"  Mean Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.mean(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# The final state was logged in the last iteration of the loop (i+1 == STEPS)\n",
        "# No need for a separate final state logging step for the ledger if the loop covers it.\n",
        "\n",
        "# Optional: Generate a final manifest for the entire ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR\n",
        "# This would be a manifest of manifests and other aggregated files.\n",
        "# print(\"\\nGenerating overall run manifest...\")\n",
        "# overall_run_manifest_hash = pt.generate_run_manifest_fingerprint(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR, copy.deepcopy(sim_params), verbose=True)\n",
        "# print(f\"Overall Run Manifest Hash: {overall_run_manifest_hash}\")"

      "source": [
        "## Summary:\n",
        "\n",
        "### Data Analysis Key Findings\n",
        "\n",
        "*   The provenance functions from the `provenance_tools.py` module, including `save_fingerprint`, `generate_run_manifest_fingerprint`, `compute_feedback_seed_adaptive`, and `log_run_to_csv_ledger`, were successfully integrated into the simulation script.\n",
        "*   The integration involved saving the provenance code to a dedicated Python file (`provenance_tools.py`), importing this module into the simulation script, and adding calls to the provenance functions within the simulation's analysis loop.\n",
        "*   Provenance logging, including generating a run manifest for output files and simulation parameters and logging a summary to a CSV ledger (`simulation_ledger.csv`), is performed for each analysis step specified by `ANALYSIS_INTERVAL`, as well as for the final simulation step.\n",
        "*   Handling of JAX `jit` compilation requirements was necessary to ensure that non-hashable provenance-related parameters did not interfere with the jitted simulation function. This was achieved by passing only JAX-compatible parameters to the jitted function while keeping the full parameter set for provenance logging.\n",
        "*   Complex number types within the simulation parameters required explicit conversion to strings before being included in the JSON-formatted section of the run manifest to avoid `TypeError: Object of type complex is not JSON serializable` during manifest generation.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Insights or Next Steps\n",
        "\n",
        "*   The implemented provenance tracking system provides a robust method for recording simulation parameters, selected outputs, and analysis results, linked by a manifest hash, enhancing the reproducibility of simulation runs.\n",
        "*   The current implementation logs provenance at fixed analysis intervals. A potential next step could involve adding logic to trigger provenance logging based on specific simulation events or criteria (e.g., detection of a collapse event) to capture transient states.\n"
      ]
    },

Include functions analyze_3d and analyze_4d that accept NumPy arrays (\"rho_final_state.npy\", \"rho_stack.npy\") and output spectral plots, SSE summaries, and Quantule classification labels (e.g., Qθ, Qν) based on SSE and field morphology."

        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "The subtask is to generate the initial structure of the `quantule_mapper.py` file. This involves creating a new code cell, using `%%writefile` to create the file, adding a docstring, imports, and placeholder function definitions as specified in the instructions.\n",

      "source": [
        "%%writefile quantule_mapper.py\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "This module implements the quantule mapping analysis for 3D and 4D simulation data.\n",
        "\n",
        "It includes functions for:\n",
        "- Multi-ray directional FFT analysis.\n",
        "- Log-prime Sum of Squared Errors (SSE) calculation.\n",
        "- Peak identification and scaling.\n",
        "- Quantule classification.\n",
        "- Analysis orchestration for 3D and 4D data.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "from scipy.signal import find_peaks\n",
        "from scipy.ndimage import map_coordinates\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Constants ---\n",
        "# Theoretical log-prime targets (k ≈ ln(p))\n",
        "LOG_PRIMES = {\n",
        "    'ln(2)': np.log(2),\n",
        "    'ln(3)': np.log(3),\n",
        "    'ln(5)': np.log(5),\n",
        "    'ln(7)': np.log(7),\n",
        "    'ln(11)': np.log(11),\n",
        "}\n",
        "\n",
        "# Quantule classification rules from blueprint\n",
        "QUANTULE_CLASSES = {\n",
        "    'Qθ': [np.log(2), np.log(3)],\n",
        "    'Qν': [np.log(5), np.log(7)],\n",
        "}\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Core Quantule Mapper Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def run_multi_ray_fft(data_field, num_rays=100, num_samples=512):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the multi-ray directional sampling protocol and computes FFT.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        data_field (np.ndarray): The 3D or 4D data field to analyze (e.g., rho).\n",
        "        num_rays (int): The number of rays to sample.\n",
        "        num_samples (int): The number of samples along each ray.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        pd.DataFrame: An aggregated power spectrum DataFrame.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Placeholder implementation\n",
        "    print(\"Running multi-ray FFT...\")\n",
        "    # This would contain the logic from the original analysis_modules.py\n",
        "    # For now, return an empty DataFrame or placeholder\n",
        "    return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def find_spectral_peaks(spectrum_df, prominence=0.1, distance=5):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Finds significant peaks in the aggregated power spectrum.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        spectrum_df (pd.DataFrame): The aggregated power spectrum.\n",
        "        prominence (float): Minimum prominence for peaks.\n",
        "        distance (int): Minimum distance between peaks.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        pd.DataFrame: DataFrame of identified peaks (wavenumber, power).\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Placeholder implementation\n",
        "    print(\"Finding spectral peaks...\")\n",
        "    # This would contain the logic from the original analysis_modules.py\n",
        "    return pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "def calculate_log_prime_sse(peaks_df):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Calculates the log-prime Sum of Squared Errors (SSE).\n",
        "\n",
        "    Aligns dominant peak to ln(2) and calculates SSE against scaled log-prime targets.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        peaks_df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame of identified peaks.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        tuple: (total_sse (float), pairs_df (pd.DataFrame)).\n",
        "               pairs_df contains original k, scaled k, target label, target k, error sq.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Placeholder implementation\n",
        "    print(\"Calculating log-prime SSE...\")\n",
        "    # This would contain the logic from the original analysis_modules.py\n",
        "    return np.inf, pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'k_scaled', 'target_label', 'target_k', 'error_sq'])\n",
        "\n",
        "def classify_quantules(pairs_df):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Classifies quantules based on their log-prime target.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        pairs_df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame from calculate_log_prime_sse.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        dict: Dictionary of DataFrames for quantule classification outputs.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Placeholder implementation\n",
        "    print(\"Classifying quantules...\")\n",
        "    # This would contain the logic from the original analysis_modules.py\n",
        "    return {\n",
        "        'sse_summary.csv': pd.DataFrame([{'sse': np.inf}]),\n",
        "        'lnprime_pairs.csv': pd.DataFrame(columns=['scaled_fft_peak', 'target_log_prime']),\n",
        "        'quantule_labels.csv': pd.DataFrame(columns=['target_log_prime', 'quantule_class'])\n",
        "    }\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Analysis Orchestration Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_3d(rho_field_path):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Runs quantule mapping analysis on a 3D rho field saved as a .npy file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        rho_field_path (str): Path to the 3D rho field .npy file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        dict: Dictionary containing analysis results (SSE, peak k, CSV data).\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"Analyzing 3D data from {rho_field_path}...\")\n",
        "    # Load data\n",
        "    rho_field = jnp.load(rho_field_path) # Assuming JAX array\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Placeholder calls to core functions\n",

        "    peaks_df = find_spectral_peaks(spectrum_df)\n",
        "    total_sse, pairs_df = calculate_log_prime_sse(peaks_df)\n",
        "    csv_outputs = classify_quantules(pairs_df)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Placeholder return structure\n",
        "    return {\n",
        "        'total_sse': total_sse,\n",
        "        'dominant_peak_k': peaks_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] if not peaks_df.empty else 0.0,\n",
        "        'csv_files': csv_outputs\n",
        "    }\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_4d(rho_stack_path):\n",

        "    Runs quantule mapping analysis on a 4D rho stack saved as a .npy file.\n",

        "    rho_stack = jnp.load(rho_stack_path) # Assuming JAX array\n",
        "\n",

        "         last_step_rho = rho_stack[-1, :, :, :]\n",
        "         return analyze_3d(last_step_rho) # Need to save last_step_rho to temp file or modify analyze_3d\n",
        "\n",
        "    print(\"Data is not 4D or empty.\")\n",
        "    return {} # Return empty dict if input is not valid 4D\n",

      "source": [
        "%%writefile -a quantule_mapper.py\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Core Quantule Mapper Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def run_multi_ray_fft(data_field, num_rays=100, num_samples=512):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the \"multi-ray directional sampling protocol.\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    Extracts 1D signals along random rays, applies a Hann window,\n",
        "    and computes the 1D FFT for each.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Ensure data_field is a numpy array for scipy functions\n",
        "    if isinstance(data_field, jnp.ndarray):\n",
        "        data_field = np.asarray(data_field)\n",
        "\n",
        "    center = np.array(data_field.shape) / 2.0\n",
        "    radius = np.min(center)\n",
        "\n",
        "    all_power_spectra = []\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Generate random ray vectors (spherical coordinates)\n",
        "    np.random.seed(42) # Use a fixed seed for reproducibility\n",
        "    phi = np.random.uniform(0, 2 * np.pi, num_rays)\n",
        "    costheta = np.random.uniform(-1, 1, num_rays)\n",
        "    theta = np.arccos(costheta)\n",
        "\n",
        "    vec_x = np.sin(theta) * np.cos(phi)\n",
        "    vec_y = np.sin(theta) * np.sin(phi)\n",
        "    vec_z = np.cos(theta)\n",
        "\n",
        "    for i in range(num_rays):\n",
        "        # Create coordinates for this ray\n",
        "        t = np.linspace(-radius, radius, num_samples)\n",
        "        x = center[0] + t * vec_x[i]\n",
        "        y = center[1] + t * vec_y[i]\n",
        "        z = center[2] + t * vec_z[i]\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Ensure coordinates are within bounds (map_coordinates handles this to some extent with mode='nearest')\n",
        "        coords = np.vstack([x, y, z])\n",
        "        # Clip coordinates to be within the bounds of the data_field\n",
        "        for dim in range(data_field.ndim):\n",
        "             coords[dim] = np.clip(coords[dim], 0, data_field.shape[dim] - 1)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Interpolate the 3D data along the 1D ray\n",
        "        # 'map_coordinates' is the standard way to do this\n",
        "        ray_signal = map_coordinates(data_field, coords, order=1, mode='nearest')\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Apply Hann window to mitigate spectral leakage\n",
        "        hann_window = np.hanning(num_samples)\n",
        "        ray_signal_windowed = ray_signal * hann_window\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Compute 1D FFT\n",
        "        fft_result = np.fft.fft(ray_signal_windowed)\n",
        "        power_spectrum = np.abs(fft_result)**2\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get frequencies (wavenumbers)\n",
        "        # We use fftfreq to get the correct frequency bins\n",
        "        # The 'd' parameter is the spatial step (sample spacing)\n",
        "        # The ray spans a distance of approximately 2*radius within the grid\n",
        "        # A more precise calculation of the effective spatial step along the ray might be needed\n",
        "        # For simplicity, we use the grid cell spacing assuming it's uniform (implicitly 1)\n",
        "        # And fftfreq computes normalized frequencies based on sample spacing\n",
        "        # Let's use the Nyquist frequency limit based on the shortest grid dimension\n",
        "        max_k_possible = np.pi / 1.0 # Assuming grid cell spacing of 1\n",
        "        wavenumbers = np.fft.fftfreq(num_samples, d=1.0) * 2 * np.pi # Convert to angular frequency if needed\n",
        "\n",
        "        # We only care about positive frequencies up to Nyquist\n",
        "        positive_mask = wavenumbers > 0\n",
        "        all_power_spectra.append(\n",
        "            pd.DataFrame({\n",
        "                'wavenumber': wavenumbers[positive_mask],\n",
        "                'power': power_spectrum[positive_mask]\n",
        "            })\n",
        "        )\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Aggregate all spectra by binning\n",
        "    # This creates a single, high-clarity spectrum\n",
        "    all_spectra_df = pd.concat(all_power_spectra)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Bin wavenumbers to aggregate power\n",
        "    # Determine appropriate bins based on the range of wavenumbers\n",
        "    # Avoid creating bins with zero width if max wavenumber is zero\n",
        "    max_wavenumber = np.max(all_spectra_df['wavenumber']) if not all_spectra_df.empty else 1.0\n",
        "    bins = np.linspace(0, max_wavenumber, num_samples // 4) # Use fewer bins for aggregation clarity\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Handle potential empty bins or issues with pd.cut\n",
        "    if len(bins) < 2: # Ensure at least one bin boundary\n",
        "         bins = np.linspace(0, max_wavenumber + 1, 2) # Create a single wide bin\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Use include_lowest=True to include the 0 boundary if needed\n",
        "    all_spectra_df['k_bin'] = pd.cut(all_spectra_df['wavenumber'], bins=bins, labels=bins[:-1], include_lowest=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Group and aggregate, handling potential empty groups\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum = all_spectra_df.groupby('k_bin')['power'].mean().reset_index()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Convert k_bin labels back to float, handling potential NaNs from empty bins\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum['wavenumber'] = aggregated_spectrum['k_bin'].astype(float)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Sort by wavenumber for plotting/analysis\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum = aggregated_spectrum.sort_values(by='wavenumber').reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    return aggregated_spectrum.dropna(subset=['wavenumber']) # Drop rows where wavenumber is NaN (shouldn't happen with valid bins)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def find_spectral_peaks(spectrum_df, prominence=0.1, distance=5):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Finds significant peaks in the aggregated power spectrum.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    if spectrum_df.empty or 'power' not in spectrum_df or 'wavenumber' not in spectrum_df or len(spectrum_df) < 2:\n",
        "        return pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'power']) # Return empty if data is insufficient\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Ensure power is a numpy array for find_peaks\n",
        "    power_values = spectrum_df['power'].values\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Handle cases where all power values are zero or near zero\n",
        "    max_power = np.max(power_values)\n",
        "    min_prominence = max_power * prominence if max_power > 1e-9 else 0\n",
        "\n",
        "    # find_peaks requires the input to be 1D\n",
        "    peaks_idx, properties = find_peaks(\n",
        "        power_values,\n",
        "        prominence=min_prominence,\n",
        "        distance=distance\n",
        "    )\n",
        "\n",
        "    if len(peaks_idx) == 0:\n",
        "         return pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'power']) # Return empty if no peaks found\n",
        "\n",
        "    peak_wavenumbers = spectrum_df['wavenumber'].iloc[peaks_idx].values\n",
        "    # Use the actual prominence values from properties\n",
        "    peak_prominences = properties['prominences']\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    return pd.DataFrame({\n",
        "        'k_peak': peak_wavenumbers,\n",
        "        'power': peak_prominences # Storing prominence as 'power' for consistency\n",
        "    }).sort_values(by='power', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True) # Sort by prominence\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def calculate_log_prime_sse(peaks_df):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Calculates the log-prime Sum of Squared Errors (SSE).\n",
        "\n",
        "    1. Finds the dominant peak and aligns it to ln(2).\n",
        "    2. Calculates the SSE for other peaks against the scaled targets.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    if peaks_df.empty or 'k_peak' not in peaks_df or peaks_df['k_peak'].max() == 0:\n",
        "        # Return infinite SSE and empty pairs_df if no valid peaks\n",
        "        return np.inf, pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'k_scaled', 'target_label', 'target_k', 'error_sq'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 1. Find dominant peak (highest power assumed to be the first row after sorting)\n",
        "    dominant_peak_k = peaks_df['k_peak'].iloc[0]\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Handle dominant peak being zero or near zero to avoid division by zero\n",
        "    if dominant_peak_k < 1e-9:\n",
        "         return np.inf, pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'k_scaled', 'target_label', 'target_k', 'error_sq'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 2. Align to ln(2) to find scaling factor 'S'\n",
        "    # S * k_dominant = ln(2)\n",
        "    scaling_factor_S = LOG_PRIMES['ln(2)'] / dominant_peak_k\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 3. Scale all observed peaks\n",
        "    peaks_df['k_scaled'] = peaks_df['k_peak'] * scaling_factor_S\n",
        "\n",
        "    lnprime_pairs = []\n",
        "    LOG_PRIME_VALUES = np.array(list(LOG_PRIMES.values()))\n",
        "    LOG_PRIME_LABELS = list(LOG_PRIMES.keys())\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 4. Find nearest log-prime target for each scaled peak\n",
        "    # Ensure scaled k values are finite before calculating errors\n",
        "    valid_scaled_k = peaks_df['k_scaled'].dropna().values\n",
        "\n",
        "    for k_scaled in valid_scaled_k:\n",
        "        # Avoid errors with non-finite scaled k values\n",
        "        if not np.isfinite(k_scaled):\n",
        "             continue\n",
        "\n",
        "        errors = np.abs(k_scaled - LOG_PRIME_VALUES)\n",
        "        best_match_idx = np.argmin(errors)\n",
        "\n",
        "        target_label = LOG_PRIME_LABELS[best_match_idx]\n",
        "        target_k = LOG_PRIME_VALUES[best_match_idx]\n",
        "        error_sq = errors[best_match_idx]**2\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Find the original k_peak that corresponds to this k_scaled value\n",
        "        original_k_peak = k_scaled / scaling_factor_S\n",
        "\n",
        "        lnprime_pairs.append({\n",
        "            'k_peak': original_k_peak, # original k\n",
        "            'k_scaled': k_scaled,\n",
        "            'target_label': target_label,\n",
        "            'target_k': target_k,\n",
        "            'error_sq': error_sq\n",
        "        })\n",
        "\n",
        "    pairs_df = pd.DataFrame(lnprime_pairs)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Remove duplicate target labels, keeping the one with the smallest error\n",
        "    if not pairs_df.empty:\n",
        "        pairs_df = pairs_df.loc[pairs_df.groupby('target_label')['error_sq'].idxmin()].reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 5. Calculate final SSE (sum of squared errors)\n",
        "    total_sse = pairs_df['error_sq'].sum()\n",
        "\n",
        "    return total_sse, pairs_df\n",
        "\n",
        "def classify_quantules(pairs_df):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Classifies quantules based on their log-prime target.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    if pairs_df.empty or 'target_label' not in pairs_df:\n",
        "        # Return empty DataFrames if input is insufficient\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'sse_summary.csv': pd.DataFrame([{'sse': np.inf, 'estimated_scaling_factor': 0.0}]),\n",
        "            'lnprime_pairs.csv': pd.DataFrame(columns=['scaled_fft_peak', 'target_log_prime']),\n",
        "            'quantule_labels.csv': pd.DataFrame(columns=['target_log_prime', 'quantule_class']),\n",
        "            'kstar_omega_heatmap.csv': pd.DataFrame([{'kstar': 0.0, 'omega': 0.0, 'power': 0.0}]) # Placeholder\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    def get_class(target_label):\n",
        "        target_k = LOG_PRIMES.get(target_label)\n",
        "        if target_k is None:\n",
        "             return 'Unclassified' # Handle case where target_label is not in LOG_PRIMES\n",
        "\n",
        "        for q_class, k_values in QUANTULE_CLASSES.items():\n",
        "            if target_k in k_values:\n",
        "                return q_class\n",
        "        return 'Unclassified'\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Ensure 'target_label' column exists before applying the function\n",
        "    if 'target_label' in pairs_df.columns:\n",
        "        pairs_df['quantule_class'] = pairs_df['target_label'].apply(get_class)\n",
        "        quantule_labels = pairs_df[['target_label', 'quantule_class']].drop_duplicates().reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        # If target_label column is missing, return empty labels df\n",
        "        quantule_labels = pd.DataFrame(columns=['target_log_prime', 'quantule_class'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create the specified output format for lnprime_pairs.csv\n",
        "    # Ensure required columns exist\n",
        "    output_lnprime_pairs = pd.DataFrame(columns=['scaled_fft_peak', 'target_log_prime'])\n",
        "    if 'k_scaled' in pairs_df.columns and 'target_label' in pairs_df.columns:\n",
        "        output_lnprime_pairs = pairs_df.rename(columns={\n",
        "            'k_scaled': 'scaled_fft_peak',\n",
        "            'target_label': 'target_log_prime'\n",
        "        })[['scaled_fft_peak', 'target_log_prime']].reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create sse_summary.csv data\n",
        "    # Recalculate total_sse here to ensure consistency, or pass it\n",
        "    total_sse = pairs_df['error_sq'].sum() if 'error_sq' in pairs_df.columns else np.inf\n",
        "    # Attempt to estimate scaling factor if peaks were found\n",
        "    estimated_scaling_factor = LOG_PRIMES['ln(2)'] / pairs_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] if not pairs_df.empty and 'k_peak' in pairs_df.columns and pairs_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] > 1e-9 else 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "    sse_summary = pd.DataFrame([{'estimated_scaling_factor': estimated_scaling_factor, 'sse': total_sse}]) # Placeholder\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create kstar_omega_heatmap.csv data\n",
        "    kstar_omega = pd.DataFrame([{'kstar': 0.0, 'omega': 0.0, 'power': 0.0}]) # Placeholder - Implementation TBD\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    return {\n",
        "        'sse_summary.csv': sse_summary,\n",
        "        'lnprime_pairs.csv': output_lnprime_pairs,\n",
        "        'quantule_labels.csv': quantule_labels,\n",
        "        'kstar_omega_heatmap.csv': kstar_omega\n",
        "    }"
      ],
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Appending to quantule_mapper.py\n"
          ]
        }
      ]

      "cell_type": "markdown",

      "source": [
        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "The previous step successfully updated `quantule_mapper.py` with the core quantule mapper functions.     {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "30062ba5",
        "outputId": "92cd7111-79d9-48dd-aa34-72da27259e67"
      },
      "source": [
        "%%writefile -a quantule_mapper.py\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Analysis Orchestration Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_3d(rho_field_path):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Runs quantule mapping analysis on a 3D rho field saved as a .npy file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        rho_field_path (str): Path to the 3D rho field .npy file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        dict: Dictionary containing analysis results (SSE, peak k, CSV data).\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"Analyzing 3D data from {rho_field_path}...\")\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Load data (can be numpy or JAX numpy)\n",
        "        rho_field = jnp.load(rho_field_path)\n",
        "        # Ensure it's 3D\n",
        "        if rho_field.ndim != 3:\n",
        "            print(f\"Error: Expected 3D data, but got {rho_field.ndim}D from {rho_field_path}\")\n",
        "            return {\n",
        "                'total_sse': np.inf,\n",
        "                'dominant_peak_k': 0.0,\n",
        "                'csv_files': classify_quantules(pd.DataFrame()) # Return empty outputs\n",
        "            }\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Convert to numpy for scipy functions used in run_multi_ray_fft\n",
        "        rho_field_np = np.asarray(rho_field)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Run Core Task 1 steps\n",
        "        spectrum_df = run_multi_ray_fft(rho_field_np)\n",
        "        peaks_df = find_spectral_peaks(spectrum_df)\n",
        "        total_sse, pairs_df = calculate_log_prime_sse(peaks_df)\n",
        "        csv_outputs = classify_quantules(pairs_df)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get dominant peak k value, handling empty peaks_df\n",
        "        dominant_peak_k = peaks_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] if not peaks_df.empty and 'k_peak' in peaks_df.columns and len(peaks_df) > 0 else 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Return all metrics and CSV dataframes\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'total_sse': float(total_sse), # Ensure float type for serialization\n",
        "            'dominant_peak_k': float(dominant_peak_k), # Ensure float type\n",
        "            'csv_files': csv_outputs\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error during 3D analysis of {rho_field_path}: {e}\")\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'total_sse': np.inf,\n",
        "            'dominant_peak_k': 0.0,\n",
        "            'csv_files': classify_quantules(pd.DataFrame()) # Return empty outputs on error\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_4d(rho_stack_path):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Runs quantule mapping analysis on a 4D rho stack saved as a .npy file.\n",
        "    (e.g., [time_steps, grid, grid, grid])\n",
        "    Analyzes the last time step of the stack.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        rho_stack_path (str): Path to the 4D rho stack .npy file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        dict: Dictionary containing analysis results for the last time step.\n",
        "              Similar structure to analyze_3d results.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"Analyzing 4D data from {rho_stack_path}...\")\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Load data (can be numpy or JAX numpy)\n",
        "        rho_stack = jnp.load(rho_stack_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Ensure it's 4D and has at least one time step\n",
        "        if rho_stack.ndim != 4 or rho_stack.shape[0] == 0:\n",
        "            print(f\"Error: Expected non-empty 4D data, but got {rho_stack.ndim}D with shape {rho_stack.shape} from {rho_stack_path}\")\n",
        "            return {\n",
        "                'total_sse': np.inf,\n",
        "                'dominant_peak_k': 0.0,\n",
        "                'csv_files': classify_quantules(pd.DataFrame()) # Return empty outputs\n",
        "            }\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Analyze the last time step (3D slice)\n",
        "        last_step_rho_np = np.asarray(rho_stack[-1, :, :, :]) # Convert last slice to numpy\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Since run_multi_ray_fft, find_spectral_peaks, etc. are designed for 3D numpy,\n",
        "        # we can call them directly with the last_step_rho_np array.\n",
        "        # Alternatively, if analyze_3d is robust, we could save the last step to a temp file and call analyze_3d.\n",
        "        # Let's call the core functions directly here to avoid temp file overhead within this function.\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Run Core Task 1 steps on the last time step\n",
        "        spectrum_df = run_multi_ray_fft(last_step_rho_np)\n",
        "        peaks_df = find_spectral_peaks(spectrum_df)\n",
        "        total_sse, pairs_df = calculate_log_prime_sse(peaks_df)\n",
        "        csv_outputs = classify_quantules(pairs_df)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get dominant peak k value, handling empty peaks_df\n",
        "        dominant_peak_k = peaks_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] if not peaks_df.empty and 'k_peak' in peaks_df.columns and len(peaks_df) > 0 else 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Return all metrics and CSV dataframes for the last time step\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'total_sse': float(total_sse), # Ensure float type\n",
        "            'dominant_peak_k': float(dominant_peak_k), # Ensure float type\n",
        "            'csv_files': csv_outputs,\n",
        "            'analyzed_time_step': rho_stack.shape[0] - 1 # Indicate which time step was analyzed\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error during 4D analysis of {rho_stack_path}: {e}\")\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'total_sse': np.inf,\n",
        "            'dominant_peak_k': 0.0,\n",
        "            'csv_files': classify_quantules(pd.DataFrame()), # Return empty outputs on error\n",
        "            'analyzed_time_step': -1 # Indicate analysis failed\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "# Example usage block (optional, for testing the module file directly)\n",
        "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
        "    print(\"Running quantule_mapper.py as a script (example usage)...\")\n",
        "    import os\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create dummy 3D data for testing analyze_3d\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting analyze_3d...\")\n",
        "    dummy_rho_3d = np.random.rand(32, 32, 32)\n",
        "    dummy_rho_3d_path = \"dummy_rho_3d.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_rho_3d_path, dummy_rho_3d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    results_3d = analyze_3d(dummy_rho_3d_path)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from 3D analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(results_3d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Clean up dummy file\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_rho_3d_path):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_rho_3d_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create dummy 4D data for testing analyze_4d\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting analyze_4d...\")\n",
        "    dummy_rho_4d = np.random.rand(10, 32, 32, 32)\n",
        "    dummy_rho_4d_path = \"dummy_rho_4d.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_rho_4d_path, dummy_rho_4d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    results_4d = analyze_4d(dummy_rho_4d_path)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from 4D analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(results_4d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Clean up dummy file\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_rho_4d_path):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_rho_4d_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Test with invalid data\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting with invalid data...\")\n",
        "    dummy_invalid_data = np.random.rand(32, 32) # 2D data\n",
        "    dummy_invalid_path = \"dummy_invalid.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_invalid_path, dummy_invalid_data)\n",
        "    invalid_results = analyze_3d(dummy_invalid_path)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from invalid data analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(invalid_results)\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_invalid_path):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_invalid_path)\n"
      ],
