      "source": [
        "## Implement multi-ray directional sampling fft\n",
        "\n",
        "### Subtask:\n",
        "Implement the multi-ray directional sampling FFT protocol within the `quantule_mapper.py` module, including handling of 3D data.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "5c41e0c4"
      },
      "source": [
        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "The subtask is to implement the multi-ray directional sampling FFT protocol within the `quantule_mapper.py` module. This involves writing the code for the `run_multi_ray_fft` function based on the provided instructions, including generating rays, interpolating data, applying a Hann window, computing the FFT, and aggregating the power spectra. I will use the `%%writefile -a` magic command to append the implementation of this function to the existing `quantule_mapper.py` file.\n",
        "\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "b2d956a3",
        "outputId": "33431f2d-3731-4eff-9b43-4663569404b7"
      },
      "source": [
        "%%writefile -a quantule_mapper.py\n",
        "\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "from scipy.ndimage import map_coordinates\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Core Quantule Mapper Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def run_multi_ray_fft(data_field, num_rays=100, num_samples=512):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the \"multi-ray directional sampling protocol.\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    Extracts 1D signals along random rays, applies a Hann window,\n",
        "    and computes the 1D FFT for each.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        data_field (np.ndarray): The 3D data field to analyze (e.g., rho).\n",
        "                                 Expected to be a NumPy array.\n",
        "        num_rays (int): The number of rays to sample.\n",
        "        num_samples (int): The number of samples along each ray.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        pd.DataFrame: An aggregated power spectrum DataFrame with 'wavenumber' and 'power' columns.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Ensure data_field is a numpy array for scipy functions\n",
        "    if isinstance(data_field, np.ndarray) is False:\n",
        "        # Attempt conversion if not already a numpy array\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            data_field = np.asarray(data_field)\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"Error converting data_field to numpy array: {e}\")\n",
        "            return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    if data_field.ndim != 3:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error: run_multi_ray_fft expects 3D data, but got {data_field.ndim}D.\")\n",
        "        return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    center = np.array(data_field.shape) / 2.0\n",
        "    # Use the minimum dimension / 2 as the maximum extent for rays\n",
        "    radius = np.min(data_field.shape) / 2.0\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    all_power_spectra = []\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Generate random ray vectors (spherical coordinates)\n",
        "    np.random.seed(42) # Use a fixed seed for reproducibility\n",
        "    phi = np.random.uniform(0, 2 * np.pi, num_rays)\n",
        "    costheta = np.random.uniform(-1, 1, num_rays)\n",
        "    theta = np.arccos(costheta)\n",
        "\n",
        "    vec_x = np.sin(theta) * np.cos(phi)\n",
        "    vec_y = np.sin(theta) * np.sin(phi)\n",
        "    vec_z = np.cos(theta)\n",
        "\n",
        "    for i in range(num_rays):\n",
        "        # Create coordinates for this ray\n",
        "        # Sample along the ray from the center outwards to the approximate edge\n",
        "        t = np.linspace(-radius, radius, num_samples)\n",
        "        x = center[0] + t * vec_x[i]\n",
        "        y = center[1] + t * vec_y[i]\n",
        "        z = center[2] + t * vec_z[i]\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Stack coordinates for map_coordinates\n",
        "        coords = np.vstack([x, y, z])\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Interpolate the 3D data along the 1D ray\n",
        "        # 'map_coordinates' handles boundary conditions using the 'mode' parameter\n",
        "        # order=1 for linear interpolation\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            ray_signal = map_coordinates(data_field, coords, order=1, mode='nearest')\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"Error during map_coordinates for ray {i}: {e}\")\n",
        "            continue # Skip this ray if interpolation fails\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Apply Hann window to mitigate spectral leakage\n",
        "        if len(ray_signal) != num_samples:\n",
        "             print(f\"Warning: ray_signal length ({len(ray_signal)}) mismatch with num_samples ({num_samples}) for ray {i}. Skipping window/FFT.\")\n",
        "             continue\n",
        "\n",
        "        hann_window = np.hanning(num_samples)\n",
        "        ray_signal_windowed = ray_signal * hann_window\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Compute 1D FFT\n",
        "        # Handle potential issues if ray_signal_windowed is not valid\n",
        "        if not np.isfinite(ray_signal_windowed).all():\n",
        "            print(f\"Warning: Ray signal contains non-finite values for ray {i}. Skipping FFT.\")\n",
        "            continue\n",
        "\n",
        "        fft_result = np.fft.fft(ray_signal_windowed)\n",
        "        power_spectrum = np.abs(fft_result)**2\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get frequencies (wavenumbers)\n",
        "        # We use fftfreq to get the correct frequency bins\n",
        "        # The 'd' parameter is the spatial step (sample spacing)\n",
        "        # The effective sample spacing along the ray depends on the ray direction and grid spacing.\n",
        "        # Assuming a uniform grid spacing of 1.0 for simplicity, the maximum wavenumber is pi.\n",
        "        # fftfreq gives frequencies from -0.5/d to 0.5/d. With d=1, this is -0.5 to 0.5 cycles/sample.\n",
        "        # Multiply by 2*pi to get angular wavenumber (radians/unit).\n",
        "        wavenumbers = np.fft.fftfreq(num_samples, d=1.0) * 2 * np.pi\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # We only care about positive frequencies\n",
        "        positive_mask = wavenumbers > 0\n",
        "        # Ensure the mask is applied correctly and results are valid\n",
        "        valid_wavenumbers = wavenumbers[positive_mask]\n",
        "        valid_power_spectrum = power_spectrum[positive_mask]\n",
        "\n",
        "        if len(valid_wavenumbers) > 0:\n",
        "             all_power_spectra.append(\n",
        "                pd.DataFrame({\n",
        "                    'wavenumber': valid_wavenumbers,\n",
        "                    'power': valid_power_spectrum\n",
        "                })\n",
        "             )\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Aggregate all spectra by binning\n",
        "    if not all_power_spectra:\n",
        "        print(\"No valid power spectra generated from rays.\")\n",
        "        return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    all_spectra_df = pd.concat(all_power_spectra)\n",
        "\n",
        "    if all_spectra_df.empty:\n",
        "         return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Bin wavenumbers to aggregate power\n",
        "    # Determine appropriate bins based on the range of wavenumbers\n",
        "    # Avoid creating bins with zero width if max wavenumber is zero or very small\n",
        "    max_wavenumber = all_spectra_df['wavenumber'].max()\n",
        "    # Choose number of bins, e.g., num_samples // 4 or a fixed number\n",
        "    num_bins = min(num_samples // 4, 100) # Cap bins to avoid too many\n",
        "    bins = np.linspace(0, max_wavenumber, num_bins)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Ensure bins has at least two elements to define intervals\n",
        "    if len(bins) < 2:\n",
        "        bins = np.array([0, max_wavenumber + 1.0]) # Create a single bin if max_k is tiny\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Use pd.cut to assign each wavenumber to a bin\n",
        "    # include_lowest=True ensures the 0 wavenumber is included in the first bin\n",
        "    all_spectra_df['k_bin'] = pd.cut(all_spectra_df['wavenumber'], bins=bins, labels=bins[:-1], include_lowest=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Group by bin and calculate the mean power\n",
        "    # Handle potential NaNs from empty bins\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum = all_spectra_df.groupby('k_bin')['power'].mean().reset_index()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Convert k_bin labels (interval left edges) back to float\n",
        "    # This might introduce NaNs if original bins labels were NaN, but with linspace from 0, this should be okay.\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum['wavenumber'] = aggregated_spectrum['k_bin'].astype(float)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Drop the intermediate k_bin column\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum = aggregated_spectrum.drop(columns=['k_bin'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Sort by wavenumber for plotting/analysis\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum = aggregated_spectrum.sort_values(by='wavenumber').reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Drop rows where wavenumber is NaN (shouldn't happen with valid bins from linspace)\n",
        "    return aggregated_spectrum.dropna(subset=['wavenumber'])\n"
      ],
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Appending to quantule_mapper.py\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "242b19cd"
      },
      "source": [
        "## Implement spectrum scaling and peak identification\n",
        "\n",
        "### Subtask:\n",
        "Implement the logic for scaling the spectrum to align the dominant peak with $k = \\ln(2)$ and for robust peak identification within the `quantule_mapper.py` module.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "5795bb12"
      },
      "source": [
        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "The subtask requires implementing the logic for scaling the spectrum and identifying peaks. The `find_spectral_peaks` function needs to be completed using `scipy.signal.find_peaks`, and the `calculate_log_prime_sse` function needs to implement the scaling and SSE calculation logic. I will write the full, corrected code for `quantule_mapper.py` incorporating the completed logic for both functions.\n",
        "\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "0c7dac42",
        "outputId": "72acf58d-9ea5-4dd4-9eaf-5a12ae404e22"
      },
      "source": [
        "%%writefile quantule_mapper.py\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "This module implements the quantule mapping analysis for 3D and 4D simulation data.\n",
        "\n",
        "It includes functions for:\n",
        "- Multi-ray directional FFT analysis.\n",
        "- Log-prime Sum of Squared Errors (SSE) calculation.\n",
        "- Peak identification and scaling.\n",
        "- Quantule classification.\n",
        "- Analysis orchestration for 3D and 4D data.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "from scipy.signal import find_peaks\n",
        "from scipy.ndimage import map_coordinates\n",
        "import os # Import os for file operations in example usage\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Constants ---\n",
        "# Theoretical log-prime targets (k ≈ ln(p))\n",
        "LOG_PRIMES = {\n",
        "    'ln(2)': np.log(2),\n",
        "    'ln(3)': np.log(3),\n",
        "    'ln(5)': np.log(5),\n",
        "    'ln(7)': np.log(7),\n",
        "    'ln(11)': np.log(11),\n",
        "}\n",
        "LOG_PRIME_VALUES = np.array(list(LOG_PRIMES.values())) # Pre-compute values for faster access\n",
        "\n",
        "# Quantule classification rules from blueprint\n",
        "QUANTULE_CLASSES = {\n",
        "    'Qθ': [np.log(2), np.log(3)],\n",
        "    'Qν': [np.log(5), np.log(7)],\n",
        "}\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Core Quantule Mapper Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def run_multi_ray_fft(data_field, num_rays=100, num_samples=512):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the \"multi-ray directional sampling protocol.\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    Extracts 1D signals along random rays, applies a Hann window,\n",
        "    and computes the 1D FFT for each.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        data_field (np.ndarray): The 3D data field to analyze (e.g., rho).\n",
        "                                 Expected to be a NumPy array.\n",
        "        num_rays (int): The number of rays to sample.\n",
        "        num_samples (int): The number of samples along each ray.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        pd.DataFrame: An aggregated power spectrum DataFrame with 'wavenumber' and 'power' columns.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Ensure data_field is a numpy array for scipy functions\n",
        "    if isinstance(data_field, np.ndarray) is False:\n",
        "        # Attempt conversion if not already a numpy array\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            data_field = np.asarray(data_field)\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"Error converting data_field to numpy array: {e}\")\n",
        "            return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    if data_field.ndim != 3:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error: run_multi_ray_fft expects 3D data, but got {data_field.ndim}D.\")\n",
        "        return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    center = np.array(data_field.shape) / 2.0\n",
        "    # Use the minimum dimension / 2 as the maximum extent for rays\n",
        "    radius = np.min(data_field.shape) / 2.0\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    all_power_spectra = []\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Generate random ray vectors (spherical coordinates)\n",
        "    np.random.seed(42) # Use a fixed seed for reproducibility\n",
        "    phi = np.random.uniform(0, 2 * np.pi, num_rays)\n",
        "    costheta = np.random.uniform(-1, 1, num_rays)\n",
        "    theta = np.arccos(costheta)\n",
        "\n",
        "    vec_x = np.sin(theta) * np.cos(phi)\n",
        "    vec_y = np.sin(theta) * np.sin(phi)\n",
        "    vec_z = np.cos(theta)\n",
        "\n",
        "    for i in range(num_rays):\n",
        "        # Create coordinates for this ray\n",
        "        # Sample along the ray from the center outwards to the approximate edge\n",
        "        t = np.linspace(-radius, radius, num_samples)\n",
        "        x = center[0] + t * vec_x[i]\n",
        "        y = center[1] + t * vec_y[i]\n",
        "        z = center[2] + t * vec_z[i]\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Stack coordinates for map_coordinates\n",
        "        coords = np.vstack([x, y, z])\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Interpolate the 3D data along the 1D ray\n",
        "        # 'map_coordinates' handles boundary conditions using the 'mode' parameter\n",
        "        # order=1 for linear interpolation\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            ray_signal = map_coordinates(data_field, coords, order=1, mode='nearest')\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"Error during map_coordinates for ray {i}: {e}\")\n",
        "            continue # Skip this ray if interpolation fails\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Apply Hann window to mitigate spectral leakage\n",
        "        if len(ray_signal) != num_samples:\n",
        "             print(f\"Warning: ray_signal length ({len(ray_signal)}) mismatch with num_samples ({num_samples}) for ray {i}. Skipping window/FFT.\")\n",
        "             continue\n",
        "\n",
        "        hann_window = np.hanning(num_samples)\n",
        "        ray_signal_windowed = ray_signal * hann_window\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Compute 1D FFT\n",
        "        # Handle potential issues if ray_signal_windowed is not valid\n",
        "        if not np.isfinite(ray_signal_windowed).all():\n",
        "            print(f\"Warning: Ray signal contains non-finite values for ray {i}. Skipping FFT.\")\n",
        "            continue\n",
        "\n",
        "        fft_result = np.fft.fft(ray_signal_windowed)\n",
        "        power_spectrum = np.abs(fft_result)**2\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get frequencies (wavenumbers)\n",
        "        # We use fftfreq to get the correct frequency bins\n",
        "        # The 'd' parameter is the spatial step (sample spacing)\n",
        "        # The effective sample spacing along the ray depends on the ray direction and grid spacing.\n",
        "        # Assuming a uniform grid spacing of 1.0 for simplicity, the maximum wavenumber is pi.\n",
        "        # fftfreq gives frequencies from -0.5/d to 0.5/d. With d=1, this is -0.5 to 0.5 cycles/sample.\n",
        "        # Multiply by 2*pi to get angular wavenumber (radians/unit).\n",
        "        wavenumbers = np.fft.fftfreq(num_samples, d=1.0) * 2 * np.pi\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # We only care about positive frequencies\n",
        "        positive_mask = wavenumbers > 0\n",
        "        # Ensure the mask is applied correctly and results are valid\n",
        "        valid_wavenumbers = wavenumbers[positive_mask]\n",
        "        valid_power_spectrum = power_spectrum[positive_mask]\n",
        "\n",
        "        if len(valid_wavenumbers) > 0:\n",
        "             all_power_spectra.append(\n",
        "                pd.DataFrame({\n",
        "                    'wavenumber': valid_wavenumbers,\n",
        "                    'power': valid_power_spectrum\n",
        "                })\n",
        "             )\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Aggregate all spectra by binning\n",
        "    if not all_power_spectra:\n",
        "        print(\"No valid power spectra generated from rays.\")\n",
        "        return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    all_spectra_df = pd.concat(all_power_spectra)\n",
        "\n",
        "    if all_spectra_df.empty:\n",
        "         return pd.DataFrame(columns=['wavenumber', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Bin wavenumbers to aggregate power\n",
        "    # Determine appropriate bins based on the range of wavenumbers\n",
        "    # Avoid creating bins with zero width if max wavenumber is zero or very small\n",
        "    max_wavenumber = all_spectra_df['wavenumber'].max()\n",
        "    # Choose number of bins, e.g., num_samples // 4 or a fixed number\n",
        "    num_bins = min(num_samples // 4, 100) # Cap bins to avoid too many\n",
        "    bins = np.linspace(0, max_wavenumber, num_bins)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Ensure bins has at least two elements to define intervals\n",
        "    if len(bins) < 2:\n",
        "        bins = np.array([0, max_wavenumber + 1.0]) # Create a single bin if max_k is tiny\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Use pd.cut to assign each wavenumber to a bin\n",
        "    # include_lowest=True ensures the 0 wavenumber is included in the first bin\n",
        "    all_spectra_df['k_bin'] = pd.cut(all_spectra_df['wavenumber'], bins=bins, labels=bins[:-1], include_lowest=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Group by bin and calculate the mean power\n",
        "    # Handle potential NaNs from empty groups\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum = all_spectra_df.groupby('k_bin')['power'].mean().reset_index()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Convert k_bin labels (interval left edges) back to float\n",
        "    # This might introduce NaNs if original bins labels were NaN, but with linspace from 0, this should be okay.\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum['wavenumber'] = aggregated_spectrum['k_bin'].astype(float)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Drop the intermediate k_bin column\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum = aggregated_spectrum.drop(columns=['k_bin'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Sort by wavenumber for plotting/analysis\n",
        "    aggregated_spectrum = aggregated_spectrum.sort_values(by='wavenumber').reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Drop rows where wavenumber is NaN (shouldn't happen with valid bins from linspace)\n",
        "    return aggregated_spectrum.dropna(subset=['wavenumber'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def find_spectral_peaks(spectrum_df, prominence=0.1, distance=5):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Finds significant peaks in the aggregated power spectrum.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        spectrum_df (pd.DataFrame): The aggregated power spectrum with 'wavenumber' and 'power'.\n",
        "        prominence (float): Minimum prominence for peaks, relative to max power.\n",
        "        distance (int): Minimum horizontal distance (in samples) between neighboring peaks.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        pd.DataFrame: DataFrame of identified peaks with 'k_peak' (wavenumber) and 'power' (prominence).\n",
        "                      Sorted by power in descending order. Returns empty DataFrame if no peaks found or invalid input.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    if spectrum_df.empty or 'power' not in spectrum_df or 'wavenumber' not in spectrum_df or len(spectrum_df) < 2:\n",
        "        # Return empty if data is insufficient (need at least 2 points to define peaks)\n",
        "        return pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Ensure power is a numpy array for find_peaks\n",
        "    power_values = spectrum_df['power'].values\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Handle cases where all power values are zero or near zero\n",
        "    max_power = np.max(power_values)\n",
        "    # Calculate minimum prominence based on a relative threshold of the maximum power\n",
        "    min_prominence = max_power * prominence if max_power > 1e-9 else 0\n",
        "\n",
        "    # find_peaks requires the input to be 1D\n",
        "    # Use the index of the spectrum_df for peak finding\n",
        "    peaks_idx, properties = find_peaks(\n",
        "        power_values,\n",
        "        prominence=min_prominence,\n",
        "        distance=distance # Distance is specified in data points (indices)\n",
        "    )\n",
        "\n",
        "    if len(peaks_idx) == 0:\n",
        "         # Return empty DataFrame if no peaks found\n",
        "         return pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'power'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Extract the wavenumber and the actual prominence for the identified peaks\n",
        "    peak_wavenumbers = spectrum_df['wavenumber'].iloc[peaks_idx].values\n",
        "    peak_prominences = properties['prominences']\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create DataFrame of peaks, sorted by prominence descending\n",
        "    peaks_df = pd.DataFrame({\n",
        "        'k_peak': peak_wavenumbers,\n",
        "        'power': peak_prominences # Store prominence as 'power' as per requirement\n",
        "    }).sort_values(by='power', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True) # Sort by prominence\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    return peaks_df\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def calculate_log_prime_sse(peaks_df):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Calculates the log-prime Sum of Squared Errors (SSE).\n",
        "\n",
        "    1. Finds the dominant peak and aligns it to ln(2).\n",
        "    2. Calculates the SSE for other peaks against the scaled targets.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        peaks_df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame of identified peaks from find_spectral_peaks.\n",
        "                                 Expected columns: 'k_peak', 'power'.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        tuple: (total_sse (float), pairs_df (pd.DataFrame)).\n",
        "               total_sse is the sum of squared errors.\n",
        "               pairs_df contains 'k_peak' (original), 'k_scaled', 'target_label', 'target_k', 'error_sq'.\n",
        "               Returns (np.inf, empty DataFrame) if no valid peaks.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Ensure peaks_df is valid and has at least one peak\n",
        "    if peaks_df.empty or 'k_peak' not in peaks_df or 'power' not in peaks_df or len(peaks_df) == 0:\n",
        "        # Return infinite SSE and empty pairs_df if no valid peaks\n",
        "        return np.inf, pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'k_scaled', 'target_label', 'target_k', 'error_sq'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 1. Find dominant peak (the one with the highest power, assuming peaks_df is sorted by power)\n",
        "    dominant_peak_k = peaks_df['k_peak'].iloc[0]\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Handle dominant peak being zero or near zero to avoid division by zero\n",
        "    if dominant_peak_k is None or not np.isfinite(dominant_peak_k) or dominant_peak_k < 1e-9:\n",
        "         return np.inf, pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'k_scaled', 'target_label', 'target_k', 'error_sq'])\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 2. Align to ln(2) to find scaling factor 'S'\n",
        "    # S * k_dominant = ln(2)\n",
        "    ln2_value = LOG_PRIMES['ln(2)']\n",
        "    if ln2_value is None or ln2_value == 0:\n",
        "         # Should not happen with LOG_PRIMES definition, but as a safeguard\n",
        "         return np.inf, pd.DataFrame(columns=['k_peak', 'k_scaled', 'target_label', 'target_k', 'error_sq'])\n",
        "\n",
        "    scaling_factor_S = ln2_value / dominant_peak_k\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 3. Scale all observed peaks\n",
        "    peaks_df['k_scaled'] = peaks_df['k_peak'] * scaling_factor_S\n",
        "\n",
        "    lnprime_pairs = []\n",
        "    LOG_PRIME_VALUES = np.array(list(LOG_PRIMES.values()))\n",
        "    LOG_PRIME_LABELS = list(LOG_PRIMES.keys())\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 4. Find nearest log-prime target for each scaled peak\n",
        "    # Ensure scaled k values are finite before calculating errors\n",
        "    valid_scaled_k_values = peaks_df['k_scaled'].dropna().values\n",
        "    valid_original_k_peaks = peaks_df.loc[peaks_df['k_scaled'].dropna().index, 'k_peak'].values\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    for idx, k_scaled in enumerate(valid_scaled_k_values):\n",
        "        # Avoid errors with non-finite scaled k values\n",
        "        if not np.isfinite(k_scaled):\n",
        "             continue\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Calculate errors between scaled k and all log-prime targets\n",
        "        errors = np.abs(k_scaled - LOG_PRIME_VALUES)\n",
        "        best_match_idx = np.argmin(errors)\n",
        "\n",
        "        target_label = LOG_PRIME_LABELS[best_match_idx]\n",
        "        target_k = LOG_PRIME_VALUES[best_match_idx]\n",
        "        error_sq = errors[best_match_idx]**2\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get the corresponding original k_peak\n",
        "        original_k_peak = valid_original_k_peaks[idx]\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        lnprime_pairs.append({\n",
        "            'k_peak': original_k_peak, # original k\n",
        "            'k_scaled': k_scaled,\n",
        "            'target_label': target_label,\n",
        "            'target_k': target_k,\n",
        "            'error_sq': error_sq\n",
        "        })\n",
        "\n",
        "    pairs_df = pd.DataFrame(lnprime_pairs)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Remove duplicate target labels, keeping the one with the smallest error\n",
        "    if not pairs_df.empty:\n",
        "        # Use idxmin to get the index of the minimum error_sq for each target_label\n",
        "        min_error_indices = pairs_df.groupby('target_label')['error_sq'].idxmin()\n",
        "        pairs_df = pairs_df.loc[min_error_indices].reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 5. Calculate final SSE (sum of squared errors)\n",
        "    # Sum the 'error_sq' column, handle empty DataFrame case\n",
        "    total_sse = pairs_df['error_sq'].sum() if 'error_sq' in pairs_df.columns and not pairs_df.empty else np.inf\n",
        "\n",
        "    return total_sse, pairs_df\n",
        "\n",
        "def classify_quantules(pairs_df):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Classifies quantules based on their log-prime target.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        pairs_df (pd.DataFrame): DataFrame from calculate_log_prime_sse\n",
        "                                 Expected columns: 'k_peak', 'k_scaled', 'target_label', 'target_k', 'error_sq'.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        dict: Dictionary of DataFrames for quantule classification outputs:\n",
        "              'sse_summary.csv', 'lnprime_pairs.csv', 'quantule_labels.csv', 'kstar_omega_heatmap.csv'.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    # Return empty DataFrames if input is insufficient or missing required columns\n",
        "    if pairs_df.empty or 'target_label' not in pairs_df or 'k_scaled' not in pairs_df or 'error_sq' not in pairs_df:\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'sse_summary.csv': pd.DataFrame([{'sse': np.inf, 'estimated_scaling_factor': 0.0}]),\n",
        "            'lnprime_pairs.csv': pd.DataFrame(columns=['scaled_fft_peak', 'target_log_prime']),\n",
        "            'quantule_labels.csv': pd.DataFrame(columns=['target_log_prime', 'quantule_class']),\n",
        "            'kstar_omega_heatmap.csv': pd.DataFrame([{'kstar': 0.0, 'omega': 0.0, 'power': 0.0}]) # Placeholder\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    def get_class(target_label):\n",
        "        target_k = LOG_PRIMES.get(target_label)\n",
        "        if target_k is None:\n",
        "             return 'Unclassified' # Handle case where target_label is not in LOG_PRIMES\n",
        "\n",
        "        for q_class, k_values in QUANTULE_CLASSES.items():\n",
        "            if target_k in k_values:\n",
        "                return q_class\n",
        "        return 'Unclassified'\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Apply classification to the 'target_label' column\n",
        "    pairs_df['quantule_class'] = pairs_df['target_label'].apply(get_class)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create the 'quantule_labels.csv' DataFrame\n",
        "    quantule_labels = pairs_df[['target_label', 'quantule_class']].drop_duplicates().reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create the specified output format for lnprime_pairs.csv\n",
        "    # Ensure required columns exist\n",
        "    output_lnprime_pairs = pairs_df.rename(columns={\n",
        "        'k_scaled': 'scaled_fft_peak',\n",
        "        'target_label': 'target_log_prime'\n",
        "    })[['scaled_fft_peak', 'target_log_prime']].reset_index(drop=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create sse_summary.csv data\n",
        "    total_sse = pairs_df['error_sq'].sum() # Sum of squared errors from the pairs_df\n",
        "    # Attempt to estimate scaling factor if a dominant peak was found (it should be if pairs_df is not empty)\n",
        "    estimated_scaling_factor = LOG_PRIMES['ln(2)'] / pairs_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] if not pairs_df.empty and 'k_peak' in pairs_df.columns and pairs_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] > 1e-9 else 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "    sse_summary = pd.DataFrame([{'estimated_scaling_factor': estimated_scaling_factor, 'sse': float(total_sse)}]) # Ensure float type\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create kstar_omega_heatmap.csv data\n",
        "    kstar_omega = pd.DataFrame([{'kstar': 0.0, 'omega': 0.0, 'power': 0.0}]) # Placeholder - Implementation TBD\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    return {\n",
        "        'sse_summary.csv': sse_summary,\n",
        "        'lnprime_pairs.csv': output_lnprime_pairs,\n",
        "        'quantule_labels.csv': quantule_labels,\n",
        "        'kstar_omega_heatmap.csv': kstar_omega\n",
        "    }\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Analysis Orchestration Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_3d(rho_field_path):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Runs quantule mapping analysis on a 3D rho field saved as a .npy file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        rho_field_path (str): Path to the 3D rho field .npy file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        dict: Dictionary containing analysis results (SSE, peak k, CSV data).\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"Analyzing 3D data from {rho_field_path}...\")\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Load data (can be numpy or JAX numpy)\n",
        "        rho_field = jnp.load(rho_field_path)\n",
        "        # Ensure it's 3D\n",
        "        if rho_field.ndim != 3:\n",
        "            print(f\"Error: Expected 3D data, but got {rho_field.ndim}D from {rho_field_path}\")\n",
        "            # Return structure matching expected output on error\n",
        "            return {\n",
        "                'total_sse': np.inf,\n",
        "                'dominant_peak_k': 0.0,\n",
        "                'csv_files': classify_quantules(pd.DataFrame()) # Return empty outputs\n",
        "            }\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Convert to numpy for scipy functions used in run_multi_ray_fft\n",
        "        rho_field_np = np.asarray(rho_field)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Run Core Task 1 steps\n",
        "        spectrum_df = run_multi_ray_fft(rho_field_np)\n",
        "        peaks_df = find_spectral_peaks(spectrum_df)\n",
        "        total_sse, pairs_df = calculate_log_prime_sse(peaks_df)\n",
        "        csv_outputs = classify_quantules(pairs_df)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get dominant peak k value, handling empty peaks_df\n",
        "        dominant_peak_k = peaks_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] if not peaks_df.empty and 'k_peak' in peaks_df.columns and len(peaks_df) > 0 else 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Return all metrics and CSV dataframes\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'total_sse': float(total_sse), # Ensure float type for serialization\n",
        "            'dominant_peak_k': float(dominant_peak_k), # Ensure float type\n",
        "            'csv_files': csv_outputs\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error during 3D analysis of {rho_field_path}: {e}\")\n",
        "        # Return structure matching expected output on error\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'total_sse': np.inf,\n",
        "            'dominant_peak_k': 0.0,\n",
        "            'csv_files': classify_quantules(pd.DataFrame()) # Return empty outputs on error\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_4d(rho_stack_path):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Runs quantule mapping analysis on a 4D rho stack saved as a .npy file.\n",
        "    (e.g., [time_steps, grid, grid, grid])\n",
        "    Analyzes the last time step of the stack.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        rho_stack_path (str): Path to the 4D rho stack .npy file.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        dict: Dictionary containing analysis results for the last time step.\n",
        "              Similar structure to analyze_3d results.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"Analyzing 4D data from {rho_stack_path}...\")\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Load data (can be numpy or JAX numpy)\n",
        "        rho_stack = jnp.load(rho_stack_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Ensure it's 4D and has at least one time step\n",
        "        if rho_stack.ndim != 4 or rho_stack.shape[0] == 0:\n",
        "            print(f\"Error: Expected non-empty 4D data, but got {rho_stack.ndim}D with shape {rho_stack.shape} from {rho_stack_path}\")\n",
        "            # Return structure matching expected output on error\n",
        "            return {\n",
        "                'total_sse': np.inf,\n",
        "                'dominant_peak_k': 0.0,\n",
        "                'csv_files': classify_quantules(pd.DataFrame()), # Return empty outputs\n",
        "                'analyzed_time_step': -1 # Indicate analysis failed\n",
        "            }\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Analyze the last time step (3D slice)\n",
        "        last_step_rho_np = np.asarray(rho_stack[-1, :, :, :]) # Convert last slice to numpy\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Since run_multi_ray_fft, find_spectral_peaks, etc. are designed for 3D numpy,\n",
        "        # we can call them directly with the last_step_rho_np array.\n",
        "        # Alternatively, if analyze_3d is robust, we could save the last step to a temp file and call analyze_3d.\n",
        "        # Let's call the core functions directly here to avoid temp file overhead within this function.\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Run Core Task 1 steps on the last time step\n",
        "        spectrum_df = run_multi_ray_fft(last_step_rho_np)\n",
        "        peaks_df = find_spectral_peaks(spectrum_df)\n",
        "        total_sse, pairs_df = calculate_log_prime_sse(peaks_df)\n",
        "        csv_outputs = classify_quantules(pairs_df)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get dominant peak k value, handling empty peaks_df\n",
        "        dominant_peak_k = peaks_df['k_peak'].iloc[0] if not peaks_df.empty and 'k_peak' in peaks_df.columns and len(peaks_df) > 0 else 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Return all metrics and CSV dataframes for the last time step\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'total_sse': float(total_sse), # Ensure float type\n",
        "            'dominant_peak_k': float(dominant_peak_k), # Ensure float type\n",
        "            'csv_files': csv_outputs,\n",
        "            'analyzed_time_step': rho_stack.shape[0] - 1 # Indicate which time step was analyzed\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error during 4D analysis of {rho_stack_path}: {e}\")\n",
        "        # Return structure matching expected output on error\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            'total_sse': np.inf,\n",
        "            'dominant_peak_k': 0.0,\n",
        "            'csv_files': classify_quantules(pd.DataFrame()), # Return empty outputs on error\n",
        "            'analyzed_time_step': -1 # Indicate analysis failed\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "# Example usage block (optional, for testing the module file directly)\n",
        "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
        "    print(\"Running quantule_mapper.py as a script (example usage)...\")\n",
        "    import os\n",
        "    import tempfile # Import tempfile for analyze_4d testing if needed\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create dummy 3D data for testing analyze_3d\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting analyze_3d...\")\n",
        "    dummy_rho_3d = np.random.rand(32, 32, 32)\n",
        "    dummy_rho_3d_path = \"dummy_rho_3d.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_rho_3d_path, dummy_rho_3d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    results_3d = analyze_3d(dummy_rho_3d_path)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from 3D analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(results_3d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Clean up dummy file\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_rho_3d_path):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_rho_3d_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create dummy 4D data for testing analyze_4d\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting analyze_4d...\")\n",
        "    dummy_rho_4d = np.random.rand(10, 32, 32, 32)\n",
        "    dummy_rho_4d_path = \"dummy_rho_4d.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_rho_4d_path, dummy_rho_4d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    results_4d = analyze_4d(dummy_rho_4d_path)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from 4D analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(results_4d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Clean up dummy file\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_rho_4d_path):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_rho_4d_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Test with invalid data (2D) for analyze_3d\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting analyze_3d with invalid data (2D)...\")\n",
        "    dummy_invalid_data_2d = np.random.rand(32, 32) # 2D data\n",
        "    dummy_invalid_path_2d = \"dummy_invalid_2d.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_invalid_path_2d, dummy_invalid_data_2d)\n",
        "    invalid_results_3d = analyze_3d(dummy_invalid_path_2d)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from invalid 2D data analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(invalid_results_3d)\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_invalid_path_2d):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_invalid_path_2d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Test with invalid data (3D but zero size) for analyze_3d\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting analyze_3d with invalid data (zero size)...\")\n",
        "    dummy_invalid_data_zero = np.random.rand(0, 32, 32) # 3D but zero size\n",
        "    dummy_invalid_path_zero = \"dummy_invalid_zero.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_invalid_path_zero, dummy_invalid_data_zero)\n",

        "    print(\"\\nTesting analyze_4d with invalid data (3D)...\")\n",
        "    dummy_invalid_data_3d_for_4d = np.random.rand(32, 32, 32) # 3D data\n",
        "    dummy_invalid_path_3d_for_4d = \"dummy_invalid_3d_for_4d.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_invalid_path_3d_for_4d, dummy_invalid_data_3d_for_4d)\n",
        "    invalid_results_4d = analyze_4d(dummy_invalid_path_3d_for_4d)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from invalid 3D data for 4D analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(invalid_results_4d)\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_invalid_path_3d_for_4d):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_invalid_path_3d_for_4d)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Test analyze_4d with empty 4D data\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting analyze_4d with empty 4D data...\")\n",
        "    dummy_empty_4d = np.random.rand(0, 32, 32, 32) # Empty 4D data\n",
        "    dummy_empty_4d_path = \"dummy_empty_4d.npy\"\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_empty_4d_path, dummy_empty_4d)\n",
        "    empty_results_4d = analyze_4d(dummy_empty_4d_path)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from empty 4D data analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(empty_results_4d)\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_empty_4d_path):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_empty_4d_path)"

      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Overwriting quantule_mapper.py\n"

    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import numpy as np, os, json, csv\n",
        "from .utils import hann, smooth_boxcar, gaussian_mixture, local_peaks\n",
        "\n",
        "LN_PRIMES = np.log(np.array([2,3,5,7,11], dtype=float))\n",
        "LN2 = float(np.log(2.0))\n",
        "\n",
        "def _ensure_dir(p):\n",
        "    os.makedirs(p, exist_ok=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "def _rfft_line(line):\n",
        "    line = line - line.mean()\n",
        "    F = np.fft.rfft(line)\n",
        "    P = (np.abs(F)**2)\n",
        "    Pn = P/ P.max() if P.max()>0 else P\n",
        "    freqs_cyc = np.fft.rfftfreq(line.size)      # cycles/voxel\n",
        "    k_rad = 2*np.pi*freqs_cyc                   # rad/voxel\n",
        "    return k_rad, Pn\n",
        "\n",
        "def _fit_S_to_ln2(k_rad, Pn):\n",
        "    if len(Pn)<3: return 1.0, None\n",
        "    sm = smooth_boxcar(Pn, 5)\n",
        "    i = int(np.argmax(sm[1:])+1)\n",
        "    kpeak = float(k_rad[i])\n",
        "    S = LN2 / kpeak if kpeak>0 else 1.0\n",
        "    return S, kpeak\n",
        "\n",
        "def _nearest(peaks_kstar, target):\n",
        "    if len(peaks_kstar)==0:\n",
        "        return None, np.inf\n",
        "    idx = int(np.argmin(np.abs(peaks_kstar - target)))\n",
        "    return float(peaks_kstar[idx]), float((peaks_kstar[idx]-target)**2)\n",
        "\n",
        "def _directional_spectra(field_w, rays=('x','y','z')):\n",
        "    X,Y,Z = field_w.shape\n",
        "    cx,cy,cz = X//2, Y//2, Z//2\n",
        "    spectra = {}\n",
        "    for r in rays:\n",
        "        if r=='x': line = field_w[:, cy, cz]\n",
        "        elif r=='y': line = field_w[cx, :, cz]\n",
        "        elif r=='z': line = field_w[cx, cy, :]\n",
        "        else:\n",
        "            raise ValueError(\"unknown basic ray\")\n",
        "        k_rad, Pn = _rfft_line(line)\n",
        "        peaks = local_peaks(Pn, thr=0.05, min_sep=2, exclude_edges=True)\n",
        "        spectra[r] = {\"k_rad\": k_rad, \"Pn\": Pn, \"peaks\": peaks}\n",
        "    return spectra\n",
        "\n",
        "def _spherical_rays(field, n_rays=60):\n",
        "    # golden-angle sampling through center; nearest-neighbor along ray\n",
        "    X,Y,Z = field.shape\n",
        "    cx,cy,cz = X//2, Y//2, Z//2\n",
        "    N = int(n_rays)\n",
        "    phi = np.pi*(3. - np.sqrt(5.))   # golden angle\n",
        "    dirs = []\n",
        "    for i in range(N):\n",
        "        z = 1 - 2*i/(N-1 if N>1 else 1)\n",
        "        r = np.sqrt(max(0.0, 1 - z*z))\n",
        "        theta = phi*i\n",
        "        x = r*np.cos(theta); y = r*np.sin(theta)\n",
        "        dirs.append((x,y,z))\n",
        "    # sample along ray\n",
        "    L = min(X,Y,Z)\n",
        "    lines = []\n",
        "    for (dx,dy,dz) in dirs:\n",
        "        xs = cx + (np.arange(L)-L//2)*dx\n",
        "        ys = cy + (np.arange(L)-L//2)*dy\n",
        "        zs = cz + (np.arange(L)-L//2)*dz\n",
        "        xs = np.clip(np.rint(xs), 0, X-1).astype(int)\n",
        "        ys = np.clip(np.rint(ys), 0, Y-1).astype(int)\n",
        "        zs = np.clip(np.rint(zs), 0, Z-1).astype(int)\n",
        "        line = field[xs, ys, zs]\n",
        "        lines.append(line)\n",
        "    return lines\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_3d(path_3d, out_dir, rays=60, window=True):\n",
        "    _ensure_dir(out_dir)\n",
        "    rho = np.load(path_3d)\n",
        "    assert rho.ndim==3, \"expected [X,Y,Z]\"\n",
        "    if window:\n",
        "        wx = hann(rho.shape[0])[:,None,None]\n",
        "        wy = hann(rho.shape[1])[None,:,None]\n",
        "        wz = hann(rho.shape[2])[None,None,:]\n",
        "        rho_w = rho * (wx*wy*wz)\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        rho_w = rho\n",
        "\n",
        "    # directional spectra (dense)\n",
        "    lines = _spherical_rays(rho_w, n_rays=rays)\n",
        "    peaks_kstar = []\n",
        "    S_best = None\n",
        "    for idx, line in enumerate(lines):\n",
        "        k_rad, Pn = _rfft_line(line)\n",
        "        if idx==0:\n",
        "            S_best, _ = _fit_S_to_ln2(k_rad, Pn)\n",
        "        # convert to k* with S_best\n",
        "        k_star = S_best * k_rad\n",
        "        # pick peaks\n",
        "        pk_idx = local_peaks(Pn, thr=0.05, min_sep=2, exclude_edges=True)\n",
        "        peaks_kstar.extend(list(k_star[pk_idx]))\n",
        "\n",
        "    # positional SSE to ln-primes\n",
        "    sse_pos = 0.0\n",
        "    pairs = []\n",
        "    for lp in LN_PRIMES:\n",
        "        nearest_k, err2 = _nearest(np.array(peaks_kstar, dtype=float), lp)\n",
        "        pairs.append({\"ln_prime\": float(lp), \"nearest_kstar\": float(nearest_k), \"squared_error\": float(err2)})\n",
        "        sse_pos += err2\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 1D curve SSE at ω≈0 analogue (use the \"best\" line 0 as representative)\n",
        "    k_rad0, Pn0 = _rfft_line(lines[0])\n",
        "    k_star0 = S_best*k_rad0\n",
        "    # restrict region and fit mixture width\n",
        "    region = (k_star0>=0) & (k_star0<= (LN_PRIMES[-1]+0.5))\n",
        "    kR = k_star0[region]; y = smooth_boxcar(Pn0[region], 5)\n",
        "    y = y/(y.max() if y.max()>0 else 1.0)\n",
        "    sigmas = np.linspace(0.01, 0.12, 56)\n",
        "    best = (1e9, None)\n",
        "    for s in sigmas:\n",
        "        m = gaussian_mixture(kR, LN_PRIMES, s)\n",
        "        sse = float(np.sum((y-m)**2))\n",
        "        if sse<best[0]: best = (sse, s)\n",
        "    sse_grid, sigma_grid = best\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 5-point SSE (sample at ln-primes from the same line)\n",
        "    def sample_nearest(x, y, x0):\n",
        "        return float(y[np.argmin(np.abs(x-x0))])\n",
        "    y_at = np.array([sample_nearest(kR, y, lp) for lp in LN_PRIMES], dtype=float)\n",
        "    y_at = y_at/(y_at.max() if y_at.max()>0 else 1.0)\n",
        "    sse_5 = float(np.sum((y_at-1.0)**2))\n",
        "\n",
        "    # quantule label (rule of thumb)\n",
        "    # presence of ln2 peak + weak side peaks -> Qθ/Qα; broad/noisy -> Qν; multi-peaks + bifurcation -> Qψ/Qd\n",
        "    qlabel = \"Qθ/α\" if y_at[0]>0.6 and y_at[1]<0.4 else (\"Qψ/Qd\" if y_at.mean()>0.5 else \"Qν\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    # save\n",
        "    import pandas as pd\n",
        "    pd.DataFrame(pairs).to_csv(os.path.join(out_dir,\"lnprime_pairs.csv\"), index=False)\n",
        "    pd.DataFrame([{\"S\":S_best,\"SSE_grid\":sse_grid,\"sigma_grid\":sigma_grid,\"SSE_5pts\":sse_5,\"SSE_positional\":sse_pos,\"quantule_label\":qlabel}]).to_csv(os.path.join(out_dir,\"sse_summary.csv\"), index=False)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # quick plot\n",
        "    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(8,4))\n",
        "    plt.plot(kR, y, label=\"Measured\")\n",
        "    from matplotlib import pyplot as _plt\n",
        "    m = gaussian_mixture(kR, LN_PRIMES, sigma_grid)\n",
        "    plt.plot(kR, m, \"--\", label=f\"Mixture σ={sigma_grid:.3f}\")\n",
        "    for lp in LN_PRIMES: plt.axvline(lp, linestyle=\":\", linewidth=0.8)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel(\"k* (IRER units)\"); plt.ylabel(\"Norm power\"); plt.legend(); plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(os.path.join(out_dir,\"radial_overlay.png\"), dpi=160)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # directional overlay\n",
        "    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(9,5))\n",
        "    for idx, line in enumerate(lines[:min(12,len(lines))]):\n",
        "        k_rad, Pn = _rfft_line(line)\n",
        "        plt.plot(S_best*k_rad, Pn/(Pn.max() if Pn.max()>0 else 1.0), alpha=0.5)\n",
        "    for lp in LN_PRIMES: plt.axvline(lp, linestyle=\":\", linewidth=0.8)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel(\"k* (IRER units)\"); plt.ylabel(\"Norm power\"); plt.title(f\"Directional overlays (first 12 rays); S={S_best:.3f}\")\n",
        "    plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(os.path.join(out_dir,\"directional_overlay.png\"), dpi=160)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "\n",
        "    return {\"S\":float(S_best), \"SSE_grid\":float(sse_grid), \"SSE_5pts\":float(sse_5), \"SSE_positional\":float(sse_pos), \"quantule_label\":qlabel}\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_4d(path_4d, out_dir, rays=48, window=True):\n",
        "    _ensure_dir(out_dir)\n",
        "    rho = np.load(path_4d)\n",
        "    assert rho.ndim==4, \"expected [T,X,Y,Z]\"\n",
        "    T,X,Y,Z = rho.shape\n",
        "    # spatial window\n",
        "    if window:\n",
        "        wx = hann(X)[:,None,None]; wy = hann(Y)[None,:,None]; wz = hann(Z)[None,None,:]\n",
        "        w3 = wx*wy*wz\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        w3 = 1.0\n",
        "    # S calibration from first frame\n",
        "    k_rad0, Pn0 = _rfft_line((rho[0]*w3).mean(axis=1)[:,Y//2]) if False else _rfft_line((rho[0]*w3)[:,Y//2,Z//2])\n",
        "    S, _ = _fit_S_to_ln2(k_rad0, Pn0)\n",
        "    # streaming temporal FFT on a set of k* bins\n",
        "    # pick rays to get directional k's, then build k* bins\n",
        "    lines = _spherical_rays((rho[0]*w3), n_rays=rays)\n",
        "    # use the first line to define k* grid\n",
        "    k_rad_line, _ = _rfft_line(lines[0])\n",
        "    k_star_line = S*k_rad_line\n",
        "    k_mask = (k_star_line>=0) & (k_star_line<= (LN_PRIMES[-1]+0.5))\n",
        "    k_star_bins = k_star_line[k_mask]\n",
        "    # temporal PSD for each k-bin (mean over rays)\n",
        "    # memory aware: compute time FFT per k-bin after collecting time series\n",
        "    import numpy as np\n",
        "    Tpsd = min(T, 512)\n",
        "    # collect time series for each bin via projection on first ray (approx)\n",
        "    series = np.zeros((k_star_bins.size, T), dtype=np.float32)\n",
        "    for t in range(T):\n",
        "        k_rad, Pn = _rfft_line((rho[t]*w3)[:,Y//2,Z//2])\n",
        "        series[:,t] = Pn[k_mask]\n",
        "    # temporal FFT\n",
        "    F = np.fft.rfft(series, axis=1)\n",
        "    P = (np.abs(F)**2)\n",
        "    # normalize per-k\n",
        "    P /= (P.max(axis=1, keepdims=True)+1e-12)\n",
        "    # save heatmap\n",
        "    import pandas as pd\n",
        "    ft = np.fft.rfftfreq(T)  # cycles/frame\n",
        "    df = pd.DataFrame({\"k_star\": np.repeat(k_star_bins, len(ft)),\n",
        "                       \"omega_norm\": np.tile(ft, k_star_bins.size),\n",
        "                       \"P_norm\": P.flatten()})\n",
        "    df.to_csv(os.path.join(out_dir,\"kstar_omega_heatmap.csv\"), index=False)\n",
        "    # quick 2D image saved by user if desired\n",
        "    return {\"S\":float(S), \"k_bins\":int(k_star_bins.size), \"T\":int(T)}\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "CMs_Dx3EzPyf"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": []
    },

      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "6a749d95"
