        "    empty_results_4d_file = analyze_4d(dummy_empty_4d_path)\n",
        "    print(\"\\nResults from empty 4D data file analysis:\")\n",
        "    print(empty_results_4d_file)\n",
        "    if os.path.exists(dummy_empty_4d_path):\n",
        "        os.remove(dummy_empty_4d_path)"
      ],
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Overwriting quantule_mapper.py\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import numpy as np, os, json, csv\n",
        "from .utils import hann, smooth_boxcar, gaussian_mixture, local_peaks\n",
        "\n",
        "LN_PRIMES = np.log(np.array([2,3,5,7,11], dtype=float))\n",
        "LN2 = float(np.log(2.0))\n",
        "\n",
        "def _ensure_dir(p):\n",
        "    os.makedirs(p, exist_ok=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "def _rfft_line(line):\n",
        "    line = line - line.mean()\n",
        "    F = np.fft.rfft(line)\n",
        "    P = (np.abs(F)**2)\n",
        "    Pn = P/ P.max() if P.max()>0 else P\n",
        "    freqs_cyc = np.fft.rfftfreq(line.size)      # cycles/voxel\n",
        "    k_rad = 2*np.pi*freqs_cyc                   # rad/voxel\n",
        "    return k_rad, Pn\n",
        "\n",
        "def _fit_S_to_ln2(k_rad, Pn):\n",
        "    if len(Pn)<3: return 1.0, None\n",
        "    sm = smooth_boxcar(Pn, 5)\n",
        "    i = int(np.argmax(sm[1:])+1)\n",
        "    kpeak = float(k_rad[i])\n",
        "    S = LN2 / kpeak if kpeak>0 else 1.0\n",
        "    return S, kpeak\n",
        "\n",
        "def _nearest(peaks_kstar, target):\n",
        "    if len(peaks_kstar)==0:\n",
        "        return None, np.inf\n",
        "    idx = int(np.argmin(np.abs(peaks_kstar - target)))\n",
        "    return float(peaks_kstar[idx]), float((peaks_kstar[idx]-target)**2)\n",
        "\n",
        "def _directional_spectra(field_w, rays=('x','y','z')):\n",
        "    X,Y,Z = field_w.shape\n",
        "    cx,cy,cz = X//2, Y//2, Z//2\n",
        "    spectra = {}\n",
        "    for r in rays:\n",
        "        if r=='x': line = field_w[:, cy, cz]\n",
        "        elif r=='y': line = field_w[cx, :, cz]\n",
        "        elif r=='z': line = field_w[cx, cy, :]\n",
        "        else:\n",
        "            raise ValueError(\"unknown basic ray\")\n",
        "        k_rad, Pn = _rfft_line(line)\n",
        "        peaks = local_peaks(Pn, thr=0.05, min_sep=2, exclude_edges=True)\n",
        "        spectra[r] = {\"k_rad\": k_rad, \"Pn\": Pn, \"peaks\": peaks}\n",
        "    return spectra\n",
        "\n",
        "def _spherical_rays(field, n_rays=60):\n",
        "    # golden-angle sampling through center; nearest-neighbor along ray\n",
        "    X,Y,Z = field.shape\n",
        "    cx,cy,cz = X//2, Y//2, Z//2\n",
        "    N = int(n_rays)\n",
        "    phi = np.pi*(3. - np.sqrt(5.))   # golden angle\n",
        "    dirs = []\n",
        "    for i in range(N):\n",
        "        z = 1 - 2*i/(N-1 if N>1 else 1)\n",
        "        r = np.sqrt(max(0.0, 1 - z*z))\n",
        "        theta = phi*i\n",
        "        x = r*np.cos(theta); y = r*np.sin(theta)\n",
        "        dirs.append((x,y,z))\n",
        "    # sample along ray\n",
        "    L = min(X,Y,Z)\n",
        "    lines = []\n",
        "    for (dx,dy,dz) in dirs:\n",
        "        xs = cx + (np.arange(L)-L//2)*dx\n",
        "        ys = cy + (np.arange(L)-L//2)*dy\n",
        "        zs = cz + (np.arange(L)-L//2)*dz\n",
        "        xs = np.clip(np.rint(xs), 0, X-1).astype(int)\n",
        "        ys = np.clip(np.rint(ys), 0, Y-1).astype(int)\n",
        "        zs = np.clip(np.rint(zs), 0, Z-1).astype(int)\n",
        "        line = field[xs, ys, zs]\n",
        "        lines.append(line)\n",
        "    return lines\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_3d(path_3d, out_dir, rays=60, window=True):\n",
        "    _ensure_dir(out_dir)\n",
        "    rho = np.load(path_3d)\n",
        "    assert rho.ndim==3, \"expected [X,Y,Z]\"\n",
        "    if window:\n",
        "        wx = hann(rho.shape[0])[:,None,None]\n",
        "        wy = hann(rho.shape[1])[None,:,None]\n",
        "        wz = hann(rho.shape[2])[None,None,:]\n",
        "        rho_w = rho * (wx*wy*wz)\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        rho_w = rho\n",
        "\n",
        "    # directional spectra (dense)\n",
        "    lines = _spherical_rays(rho_w, n_rays=rays)\n",
        "    peaks_kstar = []\n",
        "    S_best = None\n",
        "    for idx, line in enumerate(lines):\n",
        "        k_rad, Pn = _rfft_line(line)\n",
        "        if idx==0:\n",
        "            S_best, _ = _fit_S_to_ln2(k_rad, Pn)\n",
        "        # convert to k* with S_best\n",
        "        k_star = S_best * k_rad\n",
        "        # pick peaks\n",
        "        pk_idx = local_peaks(Pn, thr=0.05, min_sep=2, exclude_edges=True)\n",
        "        peaks_kstar.extend(list(k_star[pk_idx]))\n",
        "\n",
        "    # positional SSE to ln-primes\n",
        "    sse_pos = 0.0\n",
        "    pairs = []\n",
        "    for lp in LN_PRIMES:\n",
        "        nearest_k, err2 = _nearest(np.array(peaks_kstar, dtype=float), lp)\n",
        "        pairs.append({\"ln_prime\": float(lp), \"nearest_kstar\": float(nearest_k), \"squared_error\": float(err2)})\n",
        "        sse_pos += err2\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 1D curve SSE at ω≈0 analogue (use the \"best\" line 0 as representative)\n",
        "    k_rad0, Pn0 = _rfft_line(lines[0])\n",
        "    k_star0 = S_best*k_rad0\n",
        "    # restrict region and fit mixture width\n",
        "    region = (k_star0>=0) & (k_star0<= (LN_PRIMES[-1]+0.5))\n",
        "    kR = k_star0[region]; y = smooth_boxcar(Pn0[region], 5)\n",
        "    y = y/(y.max() if y.max()>0 else 1.0)\n",
        "    sigmas = np.linspace(0.01, 0.12, 56)\n",
        "    best = (1e9, None)\n",
        "    for s in sigmas:\n",
        "        m = gaussian_mixture(kR, LN_PRIMES, s)\n",
        "        sse = float(np.sum((y-m)**2))\n",
        "        if sse<best[0]: best = (sse, s)\n",
        "    sse_grid, sigma_grid = best\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 5-point SSE (sample at ln-primes from the same line)\n",
        "    def sample_nearest(x, y, x0):\n",
        "        return float(y[np.argmin(np.abs(x-x0))])\n",
        "    y_at = np.array([sample_nearest(kR, y, lp) for lp in LN_PRIMES], dtype=float)\n",
        "    y_at = y_at/(y_at.max() if y_at.max()>0 else 1.0)\n",
        "    sse_5 = float(np.sum((y_at-1.0)**2))\n",
        "\n",
        "    # quantule label (rule of thumb)\n",
        "    # presence of ln2 peak + weak side peaks -> Qθ/Qα; broad/noisy -> Qν; multi-peaks + bifurcation -> Qψ/Qd\n",
        "    qlabel = \"Qθ/α\" if y_at[0]>0.6 and y_at[1]<0.4 else (\"Qψ/Qd\" if y_at.mean()>0.5 else \"Qν\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    # save\n",
        "    import pandas as pd\n",
        "    pd.DataFrame(pairs).to_csv(os.path.join(out_dir,\"lnprime_pairs.csv\"), index=False)\n",
        "    pd.DataFrame([{\"S\":S_best,\"SSE_grid\":sse_grid,\"sigma_grid\":sigma_grid,\"SSE_5pts\":sse_5,\"SSE_positional\":sse_pos,\"quantule_label\":qlabel}]).to_csv(os.path.join(out_dir,\"sse_summary.csv\"), index=False)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # quick plot\n",
        "    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(8,4))\n",
        "    plt.plot(kR, y, label=\"Measured\")\n",
        "    from matplotlib import pyplot as _plt\n",
        "    m = gaussian_mixture(kR, LN_PRIMES, sigma_grid)\n",
        "    plt.plot(kR, m, \"--\", label=f\"Mixture σ={sigma_grid:.3f}\")\n",
        "    for lp in LN_PRIMES: plt.axvline(lp, linestyle=\":\", linewidth=0.8)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel(\"k* (IRER units)\"); plt.ylabel(\"Norm power\"); plt.legend(); plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(os.path.join(out_dir,\"radial_overlay.png\"), dpi=160)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # directional overlay\n",
        "    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(9,5))\n",
        "    for idx, line in enumerate(lines[:min(12,len(lines))]):\n",
        "        k_rad, Pn = _rfft_line(line)\n",
        "        plt.plot(S_best*k_rad, Pn/(Pn.max() if Pn.max()>0 else 1.0), alpha=0.5)\n",
        "    for lp in LN_PRIMES: plt.axvline(lp, linestyle=\":\", linewidth=0.8)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel(\"k* (IRER units)\"); plt.ylabel(\"Norm power\"); plt.title(f\"Directional overlays (first 12 rays); S={S_best:.3f}\")\n",
        "    plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(os.path.join(out_dir,\"directional_overlay.png\"), dpi=160)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "\n",
        "    return {\"S\":float(S_best), \"SSE_grid\":float(sse_grid), \"SSE_5pts\":float(sse_5), \"SSE_positional\":float(sse_pos), \"quantule_label\":qlabel}\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_4d(path_4d, out_dir, rays=48, window=True):\n",
        "    _ensure_dir(out_dir)\n",
        "    rho = np.load(path_4d)\n",
        "    assert rho.ndim==4, \"expected [T,X,Y,Z]\"\n",
        "    T,X,Y,Z = rho.shape\n",
        "    # spatial window\n",
        "    if window:\n",
        "        wx = hann(X)[:,None,None]; wy = hann(Y)[None,:,None]; wz = hann(Z)[None,None,:]\n",
        "        w3 = wx*wy*wz\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        w3 = 1.0\n",
        "    # S calibration from first frame\n",
        "    k_rad0, Pn0 = _rfft_line((rho[0]*w3).mean(axis=1)[:,Y//2]) if False else _rfft_line((rho[0]*w3)[:,Y//2,Z//2])\n",
        "    S, _ = _fit_S_to_ln2(k_rad0, Pn0)\n",
        "    # streaming temporal FFT on a set of k* bins\n",
        "    # pick rays to get directional k's, then build k* bins\n",
        "    lines = _spherical_rays((rho[0]*w3), n_rays=rays)\n",
        "    # use the first line to define k* grid\n",
        "    k_rad_line, _ = _rfft_line(lines[0])\n",
        "    k_star_line = S*k_rad_line\n",
        "    k_mask = (k_star_line>=0) & (k_star_line<= (LN_PRIMES[-1]+0.5))\n",
        "    k_star_bins = k_star_line[k_mask]\n",
        "    # temporal PSD for each k-bin (mean over rays)\n",
        "    # memory aware: compute time FFT per k-bin after collecting time series\n",
        "    import numpy as np\n",
        "    Tpsd = min(T, 512)\n",
        "    # collect time series for each bin via projection on first ray (approx)\n",
        "    series = np.zeros((k_star_bins.size, T), dtype=np.float32)\n",
        "    for t in range(T):\n",
        "        k_rad, Pn = _rfft_line((rho[t]*w3)[:,Y//2,Z//2])\n",
        "        series[:,t] = Pn[k_mask]\n",
        "    # temporal FFT\n",
        "    F = np.fft.rfft(series, axis=1)\n",
        "    P = (np.abs(F)**2)\n",
        "    # normalize per-k\n",
        "    P /= (P.max(axis=1, keepdims=True)+1e-12)\n",
        "    # save heatmap\n",
        "    import pandas as pd\n",
        "    ft = np.fft.rfftfreq(T)  # cycles/frame\n",
        "    df = pd.DataFrame({\"k_star\": np.repeat(k_star_bins, len(ft)),\n",
        "                       \"omega_norm\": np.tile(ft, k_star_bins.size),\n",
        "                       \"P_norm\": P.flatten()})\n",
        "    df.to_csv(os.path.join(out_dir,\"kstar_omega_heatmap.csv\"), index=False)\n",
        "    # quick 2D image saved by user if desired\n",
        "    return {\"S\":float(S), \"k_bins\":int(k_star_bins.size), \"T\":int(T)}\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "rdg26TOM0POg"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "\n",
        "def hann(n):\n",
        "    if n <= 1:\n",
        "        return np.ones(n, dtype=float)\n",
        "    return 0.5 - 0.5*np.cos(2*np.pi*np.arange(n)/(n-1))\n",
        "\n",
        "def smooth_boxcar(y, w=5):\n",
        "    if w <= 1:\n",
        "        return y\n",
        "    kernel = np.ones(w)/w\n",
        "    return np.convolve(y, kernel, mode=\"same\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def gaussian_mixture(kgrid, centers, sigma):\n",
        "    g = np.zeros_like(kgrid, dtype=float)\n",
        "    for c in centers:\n",
        "        g += np.exp(-0.5*((kgrid - c)/sigma)**2)\n",
        "    mx = g.max()\n",
        "    if mx>0: g/=mx\n",
        "    return g\n",
        "\n",
        "def local_peaks(y, thr=0.05, min_sep=2, exclude_edges=True):\n",
        "    idxs = []\n",
        "    n = len(y)\n",
        "    rng = range(1, n-1) if exclude_edges else range(0,n)\n",
        "    for i in rng:\n",
        "        yl = y[i-1] if i>0 else -1e9\n",
        "        yr = y[i+1] if i<n-1 else -1e9\n",
        "        if y[i] >= yl and y[i] >= yr and y[i] > thr:\n",
        "            idxs.append(i)\n",
        "    # sort by amplitude, enforce separation\n",
        "    idxs = sorted(idxs, key=lambda i: y[i], reverse=True)\n",
        "    kept = []\n",
        "    for i in idxs:\n",
        "        if not kept or all(abs(i-j)>=min_sep for j in kept):\n",
        "            kept.append(i)\n",
        "    return kept\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "\n",
        "def hann(n):\n",
        "    if n <= 1:\n",
        "        return np.ones(n, dtype=float)\n",
        "    return 0.5 - 0.5*np.cos(2*np.pi*np.arange(n)/(n-1))\n",
        "\n",
        "def smooth_boxcar(y, w=5):\n",
        "    if w <= 1:\n",
        "        return y\n",
        "    kernel = np.ones(w)/w\n",
        "    return np.convolve(y, kernel, mode=\"same\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def gaussian_mixture(kgrid, centers, sigma):\n",
        "    g = np.zeros_like(kgrid, dtype=float)\n",
        "    for c in centers:\n",
        "        g += np.exp(-0.5*((kgrid - c)/sigma)**2)\n",
        "    mx = g.max()\n",
        "    if mx>0: g/=mx\n",
        "    return g\n",
        "\n",
        "def local_peaks(y, thr=0.05, min_sep=2, exclude_edges=True):\n",
        "    idxs = []\n",
        "    n = len(y)\n",
        "    rng = range(1, n-1) if exclude_edges else range(0,n)\n",
        "    for i in rng:\n",
        "        yl = y[i-1] if i>0 else -1e9\n",
        "        yr = y[i+1] if i<n-1 else -1e9\n",
        "        if y[i] >= yl and y[i] >= yr and y[i] > thr:\n",
        "            idxs.append(i)\n",
        "    # sort by amplitude, enforce separation\n",
        "    idxs = sorted(idxs, key=lambda i: y[i], reverse=True)\n",
        "    kept = []\n",
        "    for i in idxs:\n",
        "        if not kept or all(abs(i-j)>=min_sep for j in kept):\n",
        "            kept.append(i)\n",
        "    return kept\n"

    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "# Advanced Integrated Simulation Data Analysis Script (v6 - Memory Efficient)\n",
        "#\n",
        "# This script performs a comprehensive, memory-efficient analysis of IRER\n",
        "# simulation data by processing the combined CSV in chunks.\n",
        "#\n",
        "# Required Libraries:\n",
        "# pip install pandas matplotlib seaborn scipy tqdm\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "import seaborn as sns\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import os\n",
        "import re\n",
        "from scipy.signal import correlate, find_peaks\n",
        "from scipy.fft import rfft, rfftfreq\n",
        "from scipy.optimize import curve_fit\n",
        "from tqdm import tqdm\n",
        "\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "# SECTION 1: DATA LOADING & PARAMETER EXTRACTION\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "\n",
        "def load_and_process_data_in_chunks(filepath):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Loads and processes the combined simulation data from a CSV file in chunks\n",
        "    to remain memory-efficient. It returns two DataFrames: one with all collapse\n",
        "    events and another with only the final state of each simulation run.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"🔄 Loading and processing data from '{filepath}' in chunks...\")\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        dtypes = {\n",
        "            'time': 'int32', 'index': 'int32', 'rho_before_reset': 'float32',\n",
        "            'rho_after_reset': 'float32', 'source_directory': 'object'\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Get total lines for an accurate progress bar\n",
        "        with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n",
        "            total_lines = sum(1 for _ in f) - 1\n",
        "\n",
        "        chunk_size = 200000  # Process in chunks of 200,000 rows\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Initialize lists to store processed data from chunks\n",
        "        all_events_chunks = []\n",
        "        potential_final_states = []\n",
        "\n",
        "        with pd.read_csv(filepath, dtype=dtypes, chunksize=chunk_size, encoding='utf-8') as reader:\n",
        "            for chunk in tqdm(reader, total=int(np.ceil(total_lines / chunk_size)), desc=\"Processing CSV Chunks\"):\n",
        "                # Extract parameters and add them as new columns to the chunk\n",
        "                param_cols = chunk['source_directory'].apply(extract_params_from_foldername)\n",
        "                chunk = pd.concat([chunk, param_cols], axis=1)\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Append the entire chunk to the list for all events\n",
        "                all_events_chunks.append(chunk.copy())\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Identify the last known entry for each simulation run within this chunk\n",
        "                potential_final_states.append(chunk.groupby('source_directory').tail(1))\n",
        "\n",
        "        print(\"✅ Chunks loaded. Finalizing DataFrames...\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Concatenate all chunks to create the full events DataFrame\n",
        "        all_events_df = pd.concat(all_events_chunks, ignore_index=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Concatenate all potential final states and find the true final state for each run\n",
        "        final_states_df = pd.concat(potential_final_states, ignore_index=True)\n",
        "        final_states_df = final_states_df.loc[final_states_df.groupby('source_directory')['time'].idxmax()]\n",
        "\n",
        "        print(f\"✅ Data processed successfully. Found {len(all_events_df):,} total events and {len(final_states_df):,} unique runs.\")\n",
        "        return final_states_df, all_events_df\n",
        "\n",
        "    except FileNotFoundError:\n",
        "        print(f\"❌ ERROR: File not found at '{filepath}'. Please check the path.\")\n",
        "        return None, None\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"❌ An error occurred while loading the file: {e}\")\n",
        "        return None, None\n",
        "\n",
        "def extract_params_from_foldername(folder_name):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Extracts multiple simulation parameters from a folder name string.\"\"\"\n",
        "    params = {\n",
        "        'k_value': np.nan, 'D_value': np.nan, 'N_value': np.nan,\n",
        "        'threshold_value': np.nan, 'splash_fraction_value': np.nan\n",
        "    }\n",
        "    folder_name = str(folder_name)\n",
        "\n",
        "    patterns = {\n",
        "        'k_value': r'kS(\\d+\\.\\d+)',\n",
        "        'D_value': r'D(\\d+\\.\\d+)',\n",
        "        'N_value': r'N(\\d+)',\n",
        "        'threshold_value': r'Thresh(\\d+\\.\\d+)',\n",
        "        'splash_fraction_value': r'sf(\\d+\\.\\d+)'\n",
        "    }\n",
        "    for key, pattern in patterns.items():\n",
        "        match = re.search(pattern, folder_name)\n",
        "        if match:\n",
        "            params[key] = float(match.group(1))\n",
        "    return pd.Series(params)\n",
        "\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "# SECTION 2: STATISTICAL & BASIC PLOTTING\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "\n",
        "def print_statistics(df):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Calculates and prints a statistical summary.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"\\n--- Statistical Summary for 'rho_before_reset' ---\")\n",
        "    print(df['rho_before_reset'].describe())\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_overall_rho_distribution(df, output_folder):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Creates a histogram of all rho_before_reset values.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"\\n📊 Generating overall rho distribution histogram...\")\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))\n",
        "    sns.histplot(df['rho_before_reset'].dropna(), bins=100, kde=True)\n",
        "    plt.title('Overall Distribution of Rho Values Before Reset', fontsize=16)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel('rho_before_reset Value', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.ylabel('Frequency', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.grid(alpha=0.5)\n",
        "    plt.savefig(os.path.join(output_folder, '1_overall_rho_distribution.png'), dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(\"✅ Plot saved.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_final_rho_distribution(df, output_folder):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Creates a histogram of the final rho value for each simulation.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"\\n📊 Generating histogram for FINAL rho values...\")\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))\n",
        "    sns.histplot(df['rho_before_reset'].dropna(), bins=100, kde=True, color='green')\n",
        "    plt.title('Distribution of FINAL Rho Values (One per Simulation)', fontsize=16)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel('Final rho_before_reset Value', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.ylabel('Number of Simulations', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.grid(alpha=0.5)\n",
        "    plt.savefig(os.path.join(output_folder, '2_final_rho_distribution.png'), dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(f\"✅ Plot saved.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_rho_vs_k_scatter(final_states, output_folder):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Creates a scatter plot of final rho value vs. k value.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"\\n📊 Generating scatter plot for Final Rho vs. K value...\")\n",
        "    plot_df = final_states.dropna(subset=['k_value'])\n",
        "    if plot_df.empty:\n",
        "        print(\"  - Skipping: No data with valid k_values found.\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))\n",
        "    sns.scatterplot(\n",
        "        x='k_value',\n",
        "        y='rho_before_reset',\n",
        "        data=plot_df,\n",
        "        alpha=0.7,\n",
        "        s=50,\n",
        "        color='darkorange'\n",
        "    )\n",
        "    plt.title('Final Rho Value vs. K Value', fontsize=16)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel('k Value', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.ylabel('Final rho_before_reset Value', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.5)\n",
        "    output_filename = os.path.join(output_folder, '3_rho_vs_k_scatter.png')\n",
        "    plt.savefig(output_filename, dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(f\"✅ Plot saved as '{output_filename}'\")\n",
        "\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "# SECTION 3: ADVANCED ANALYSIS FUNCTIONS\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_spatial_autocorrelation(rho_slice, title, output_folder):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Analyzes and plots the spatial autocorrelation of a 1D rho slice.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n📊 Plotting spatial autocorrelation for: {title}...\")\n",
        "    if rho_slice is None or len(rho_slice) < 3:\n",
        "        print(\"  - Skipping: rho slice is invalid or too short.\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "\n",
        "    rho_normalized = (rho_slice - np.mean(rho_slice)) / np.std(rho_slice)\n",
        "    autocorr_full = correlate(rho_normalized, rho_normalized, mode='full')\n",
        "    zero_lag_index = (len(autocorr_full) - 1) // 2\n",
        "    positive_lags_acf = autocorr_full[zero_lag_index:] / autocorr_full[zero_lag_index]\n",
        "\n",
        "    positive_lags_indices = np.arange(len(rho_slice))\n",
        "    dips_indices, _ = find_peaks(-positive_lags_acf[1:], prominence=0.01)\n",
        "\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))\n",
        "    plt.plot(positive_lags_indices, positive_lags_acf, label='ACF')\n",
        "\n",
        "    if len(dips_indices) > 0:\n",
        "        first_dip_lag = positive_lags_indices[dips_indices[0] + 1]\n",
        "        plt.scatter([first_dip_lag], [positive_lags_acf[first_dip_lag]],\n",
        "                    c='red', marker='v', s=80, label=f\"1st Dip (Lag {first_dip_lag})\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    plt.title(f'Spatial Autocorrelation Function (ACF) - {title}', fontsize=16)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel('Lag (cells)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.ylabel('Autocorrelation', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.grid(True, linestyle=':', alpha=0.7)\n",
        "    plt.legend()\n",
        "    plt.xlim(0, len(rho_slice) // 2)\n",
        "    plt.ylim(min(-0.5, np.min(positive_lags_acf) if positive_lags_acf.size > 0 else -0.5), 1.05)\n",
        "\n",
        "    output_filename = os.path.join(output_folder, f\"4_spatial_autocorrelation_{title.replace(' ', '_')}.png\")\n",
        "    plt.savefig(output_filename, dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(f\"✅ Plot saved as '{output_filename}'\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_collapse_influence_heatmap(df, output_folder, max_temporal_lag=10, max_spatial_lag=10):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Creates a 2D heatmap of collapse event influence based on dt and dx.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"\\n📊 Generating Collapse Influence Heatmap...\")\n",
        "    events = df[['time', 'index', 'source_directory']].to_numpy()\n",
        "    if events.shape[0] < 2:\n",
        "        print(\"  - Skipping: Not enough collapse events to analyze influence.\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "\n",
        "    num_cells_series = df['N_value'].dropna()\n",
        "    num_cells = int(num_cells_series.iloc[0]) if not num_cells_series.empty else 128\n",
        "\n",
        "    dt_list, dx_list = [], []\n",
        "    for i in tqdm(range(len(events)), desc=\"Calculating Collapse Influence\"):\n",
        "        t1, c1, _ = events[i]\n",
        "\n",
        "        t2_all = events[i + 1:, 0]\n",
        "        c2_all = events[i + 1:, 1]\n",
        "\n",
        "        if not len(t2_all): continue\n",
        "\n",
        "        dt_candidates = t2_all - t1\n",
        "        valid_dt_mask = (dt_candidates > 0) & (dt_candidates <= max_temporal_lag)\n",
        "        if not np.any(valid_dt_mask): continue\n",
        "\n",
        "        dt_values = dt_candidates[valid_dt_mask]\n",
        "        c2_values_filtered = c2_all[valid_dt_mask]\n",
        "\n",
        "        dc_direct = np.abs(c1 - c2_values_filtered)\n",
        "        dx_values = np.minimum(dc_direct, num_cells - dc_direct)\n",
        "\n",
        "        valid_dx_mask = dx_values <= max_spatial_lag\n",
        "        dt_list.extend(dt_values[valid_dx_mask])\n",
        "        dx_list.extend(dx_values[valid_dx_mask])\n",
        "\n",
        "    if not dt_list:\n",
        "        print(\"  - Skipping: No correlated collapse pairs found within the specified lags.\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "\n",
        "    time_bins = np.arange(0.5, max_temporal_lag + 1.6, 1)\n",
        "    space_bins = np.arange(-0.5, max_spatial_lag + 1.6, 1)\n",
        "    counts_2d_hist, _, _ = np.histogram2d(dt_list, dx_list, bins=[time_bins, space_bins])\n",
        "\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))\n",
        "    sns.heatmap(counts_2d_hist.T, annot=True, fmt='.0f', cmap='viridis',\n",
        "                xticklabels=np.arange(1, max_temporal_lag + 1),\n",
        "                yticklabels=np.arange(0, max_spatial_lag + 1),\n",
        "                cbar_kws={'label': 'Number of Correlated Pairs'})\n",
        "    plt.title('Collapse Influence Heatmap', fontsize=16)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel('Time Difference (dt) between collapses (timesteps)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.ylabel('Spatial Distance (dx) between collapses (cells)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.gca().invert_yaxis()\n",
        "    output_filename = os.path.join(output_folder, '5_collapse_influence_heatmap.png')\n",
        "    plt.savefig(output_filename, dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(f\"✅ Plot saved as '{output_filename}'\")\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_spatial_fft_spectrum(rho_slice, k_target, output_folder, title_suffix):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Performs FFT analysis on a spatial slice and plots the power spectrum.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n📊 Generating Spatial FFT Power Spectrum for: {title_suffix}...\")\n",
        "    if rho_slice is None or len(rho_slice) < 2:\n",
        "        print(\"  - Skipping: Rho slice is invalid or too short for FFT.\")\n",
        "        return np.nan\n",
        "\n",
        "    N_points = len(rho_slice)\n",
        "    data_detrended = rho_slice - np.mean(rho_slice)\n",
        "    yf_coeffs = rfft(data_detrended)\n",
        "    xf_freqs = rfftfreq(N_points, d=1.0)\n",
        "    power_spectrum = (np.abs(yf_coeffs)**2) / N_points\n",
        "\n",
        "    xf_freqs_no_dc = xf_freqs[1:]\n",
        "    power_spectrum_no_dc = power_spectrum[1:]\n",
        "\n",
        "    k_peak_val = np.nan\n",
        "    if len(power_spectrum_no_dc) > 0:\n",
        "        peak_index_in_no_dc_spectrum = np.argmax(power_spectrum_no_dc)\n",
        "        peak_frequency = xf_freqs_no_dc[peak_index_in_no_dc_spectrum]\n",
        "        k_peak_val = 2 * np.pi * peak_frequency\n",
        "\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))\n",
        "    plt.plot(xf_freqs_no_dc * 2 * np.pi, power_spectrum_no_dc, label=\"Power Spectrum\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    if not np.isnan(k_peak_val):\n",
        "        plt.scatter([k_peak_val], [power_spectrum_no_dc[peak_index_in_no_dc_spectrum]],\n",
        "                    color='red', s=50, zorder=5, label=f'Peak $k$={k_peak_val:.3f}')\n",
        "\n",
        "    if k_target is not None:\n",
        "        plt.axvline(k_target, color='green', linestyle='--', label=f'Target $k$={k_target:.3f}')\n",
        "\n",
        "    plt.title(f'Spatial FFT Power Spectrum - {title_suffix}', fontsize=16)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel(\"Wavenumber $k$ (radians/cell)\", fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.ylabel(\"Power Spectral Density\", fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.legend()\n",
        "    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.5)\n",
        "\n",
        "    output_filename = os.path.join(output_folder, f\"6_spatial_fft_spectrum_{title_suffix.replace(' ', '_')}.png\")\n",
        "    plt.savefig(output_filename, dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(f\"✅ Plot saved as '{output_filename}'\")\n",
        "    return k_peak_val\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_quantule_features(rho_history):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Calculates feature metrics from a rho_history array for Quantule classification.\"\"\"\n",
        "    if rho_history is None or rho_history.size == 0:\n",
        "        return {'mean_field_density': np.nan, 'central_collapse_density': np.nan, 'field_std_dev': np.nan}\n",
        "\n",
        "    if rho_history.shape[0] > 50:\n",
        "        rho_slice = np.mean(rho_history[-50:, :], axis=0)\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        rho_slice = rho_history[-1, :]\n",
        "\n",
        "    mean_density = np.mean(rho_slice)\n",
        "    std_dev = np.std(rho_slice)\n",
        "\n",
        "    center_start = int(len(rho_slice) * 0.45)\n",
        "    center_end = int(len(rho_slice) * 0.55)\n",
        "    central_density = np.mean(rho_slice[center_start:center_end])\n",
        "\n",
        "    return {\n",
        "        'mean_field_density': mean_density,\n",
        "        'central_collapse_density': central_density,\n",
        "        'field_std_dev': std_dev\n",
        "    }\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_quantule_feature_map(feature_df, output_folder):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Generates the Quantule Pattern Feature Map scatter plot.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"\\n📊 Generating Quantule Pattern Feature Map...\")\n",
        "    if feature_df.empty:\n",
        "        print(\"  - Skipping: No feature data to plot.\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))\n",
        "    sns.scatterplot(\n",
        "        data=feature_df,\n",
        "        x='mean_field_density',\n",
        "        y='central_collapse_density',\n",
        "        size='field_std_dev',\n",
        "        hue='field_std_dev',\n",
        "        palette='viridis',\n",
        "        sizes=(50, 500),\n",
        "        alpha=0.7\n",
        "    )\n",
        "    plt.title('Quantule Pattern Feature Map', fontsize=16)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel('Mean Field Density (Coherence)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.ylabel('Central Collapse Density (Stability)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.6)\n",
        "    plt.legend(title='Field Std Dev (Fragmentation)')\n",
        "    output_filename = os.path.join(output_folder, '7_quantule_feature_map.png')\n",
        "    plt.savefig(output_filename, dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(f\"✅ Plot saved as '{output_filename}'\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_k_vs_parameter(df, param_name, output_folder):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Plots k_peak vs. a given parameter, like Diffusion.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n📊 Generating k_peak vs. {param_name} plot...\")\n",
        "    plot_df = df.dropna(subset=['k_peak', param_name])\n",
        "    if plot_df.empty:\n",
        "        print(f\"  - Skipping: No data available for k_peak vs {param_name}.\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "\n",
        "    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))\n",
        "    sns.lineplot(data=plot_df, x=param_name, y='k_peak', marker='o', color='teal')\n",
        "    plt.axhline(np.log(2), color='red', linestyle='--', label=f'k = ln(2) ≈ {np.log(2):.4f}')\n",
        "    plt.title(f'Collapse Spectrum: $k_{{peak}}$ vs. {param_name}', fontsize=16)\n",
        "    plt.xlabel(f'{param_name} Coefficient', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.ylabel('Spectral Peak $k$', fontsize=12)\n",
        "    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.6)\n",
        "    plt.legend()\n",
        "    output_filename = os.path.join(output_folder, f'8_k_peak_vs_{param_name}.png')\n",
        "    plt.savefig(output_filename, dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(f\"✅ Plot saved as '{output_filename}'\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def plot_3d_pas_field_slice(field_data, title, output_folder, filename):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Generates a 2D slice visualization of a 3D PAS collapse field.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"\\n🎨 Generating 2D slice for 3D PAS field: {title}...\")\n",
        "    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))\n",
        "    fig.suptitle(f'3D PAS Collapse Field - {title}', fontsize=16)\n",
        "\n",
        "    planes = {'X-Y Plane (Z=0)': field_data[:, :, field_data.shape[2] // 2],\n",
        "              'X-Z Plane (Y=0)': field_data[:, field_data.shape[1] // 2, :],\n",
        "              'Y-Z Plane (X=0)': field_data[field_data.shape[0] // 2, :, :]}\n",
        "\n",
        "    for ax, (plane_title, plane_data) in zip(axes, planes.items()):\n",
        "        ax.imshow(plane_data.T, cmap='viridis', origin='lower', extent=[-5, 5, -5, 5])\n",
        "        ax.set_title(plane_title, fontsize=14)\n",
        "        ax.set_xlabel('Position')\n",
        "        ax.set_ylabel('Position')\n",
        "        ax.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.4)\n",
        "\n",
        "    plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0.03, 1, 0.95])\n",
        "    output_filename = os.path.join(output_folder, filename)\n",
        "    plt.savefig(output_filename, dpi=150)\n",
        "    plt.close()\n",
        "    print(f\"✅ Plot saved as '{output_filename}'\")\n",
        "\n",
        "def power_law(x, a, b, c):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Power law function for curve fitting: a * |x - c|^b\"\"\"\n",
        "    return a * np.power(np.abs(x - c), b)\n",
        "\n",
        "def analyze_critical_exponents(df, output_folder):\n",
        "    \"\"\"Finds critical points and fits power laws to extract exponents.\"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"\\n🔬 Performing Critical Exponent Analysis...\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    if 'threshold_value' not in df.columns or df['threshold_value'].isnull().all():\n",
        "        print(\"  - Skipping: 'threshold_value' column not found or is empty.\")\n",
        "        return {}\n",
        "\n",
        "    param_df = df.dropna(subset=['threshold_value', 'rho_before_reset']).groupby('threshold_value').agg(\n",
        "        avg_rho=('rho_before_reset', 'mean')\n",
        "    ).reset_index()\n",
        "\n",
        "    if len(param_df) < 3:\n",
        "        print(\"  - Skipping: Not enough data points to determine a gradient.\")\n",
        "        return {}\n",
        "\n",
        "    param_df['rho_gradient'] = np.gradient(param_df['avg_rho'])\n",
        "    gc = param_df.loc[param_df['rho_gradient'].idxmax()]['threshold_value']\n",
        "    print(f\"  - Estimated Critical Threshold (gc): {gc:.4f}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        popt_beta, _ = curve_fit(power_law, param_df['threshold_value'], param_df['avg_rho'], p0=[1, 0.5, gc], maxfev=5000)\n",
        "        beta = popt_beta[1]\n",
        "        print(f\"  - Extracted Critical Exponent (β): {beta:.4f}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
        "        plt.scatter(param_df['threshold_value'], param_df['avg_rho'], label='Simulation Data')\n",
        "        plt.plot(param_df['threshold_value'], power_law(param_df['threshold_value'], *popt_beta), color='red', label=f'Fit: β ≈ {beta:.3f}')\n",
        "        plt.axvline(gc, color='gray', linestyle='--', label=f'Critical Point $g_c$ ≈ {gc:.3f}')\n",
        "        plt.title('Power-Law Fit for Order Parameter (β)', fontsize=16)\n",
        "        plt.xlabel('Tuning Parameter (threshold)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "        plt.ylabel('Order Parameter (avg_rho_final)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "        plt.legend()\n",
        "        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.5)\n",
        "        plt.savefig(os.path.join(output_folder, '9_critical_exponent_beta_fit.png'), dpi=150)\n",
        "        plt.close()\n",
        "        print(\"  ✅ Beta exponent plot saved.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    except (RuntimeError, ValueError) as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"  - WARNING: Could not converge on a fit for the beta exponent. Error: {e}\")\n",
        "        beta = np.nan\n",
        "\n",
        "    return {'gc': gc, 'beta': beta}\n",
        "\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "# SECTION 5: MAIN EXECUTION\n",
        "# ==============================================================================\n",
        "\n",
        "def main():\n",
        "    \"\"\"Main function to run the full analysis pipeline.\"\"\"\n",
        "    input_csv_path = 'combined_collapse_data.csv'\n",
        "    output_plot_folder = 'analysis_plots'\n",
        "\n",
        "    if not os.path.exists(input_csv_path):\n",
        "        print(f\"❌ ERROR: '{input_csv_path}' not found. Please run the data extraction script first.\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "\n",
        "    os.makedirs(output_plot_folder, exist_ok=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Process data in a memory-efficient way\n",
        "    final_states_df, all_events_df = load_and_process_data_in_chunks(input_csv_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "    if final_states_df is not None and all_events_df is not None:\n",
        "\n",
        "        print_statistics(all_events_df)\n",
        "        plot_overall_rho_distribution(all_events_df, output_plot_folder)\n",
        "        plot_final_rho_distribution(final_states_df, output_plot_folder)\n",
        "        plot_rho_vs_k_scatter(final_states_df, output_plot_folder)\n",
        "        plot_collapse_influence_heatmap(all_events_df, output_plot_folder)\n",
        "\n",
        "        unique_runs = final_states_df['source_directory'].unique()\n",
        "        runs_to_analyze = []\n",
        "        if len(unique_runs) > 0: runs_to_analyze.append((\"First_Run\", unique_runs[0]))\n",
        "        if len(unique_runs) > 1: runs_to_analyze.append((\"Last_Run\", unique_runs[-1]))\n",
        "\n",
        "        quantule_features = []\n",
        "        k_peak_data = []\n",
        "\n",
        "        for title, run_dir in tqdm(runs_to_analyze, desc=\"Advanced Run Analysis\"):\n",
        "            print(f\"\\n--- Running Advanced Analysis on: {title} ({run_dir}) ---\")\n",
        "            rho_history_path = os.path.join(run_dir, 'rho_history.npy')\n",
        "            try:\n",
        "                rho_history = np.load(rho_history_path)\n",
        "                rho_slice = np.mean(rho_history[-50:, :], axis=0) if rho_history.shape[0] > 50 else rho_history[-1, :]\n",
        "\n",
        "                plot_spatial_autocorrelation(rho_slice, title, output_folder)\n",
        "                k_peak = plot_spatial_fft_spectrum(rho_slice, k_target=np.log(2), output_folder=output_plot_folder, title_suffix=title)\n",
        "\n",
        "                run_params = extract_params_from_foldername(run_dir)\n",
        "                k_peak_data.append({'k_peak': k_peak, 'D_value': run_params['D_value']})\n",
        "\n",
        "                features = analyze_quantule_features(rho_history)\n",
        "                features['run_name'] = run_dir\n",
        "                quantule_features.append(features)\n",
        "\n",
        "            except FileNotFoundError:\n",
        "                print(f\"  - WARNING: 'rho_history.npy' not found in '{run_dir}'. Skipping advanced analysis.\")\n",
        "            except Exception as e:\n",
        "                print(f\"  - ERROR: Could not process rho_history for '{run_dir}'. Error: {e}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        if quantule_features:\n",
        "            feature_df = pd.DataFrame(quantule_features)\n",
        "            plot_quantule_feature_map(feature_df, output_plot_folder)\n",
        "\n",
        "        if k_peak_data and 'D_value' in k_peak_data[0]:\n",
        "            k_peak_df = pd.DataFrame(k_peak_data)\n",
        "            plot_k_vs_parameter(k_peak_df, 'D_value', output_plot_folder)\n",
        "\n",
        "        analyze_critical_exponents(final_states_df, output_plot_folder)\n",
        "\n",
        "        grid_size = 64\n",
        "        x, y, z = np.mgrid[-5:5:grid_size*1j, -5:5:grid_size*1j, -5:5:grid_size*1j]\n",
        "\n",
        "        q_theta_field = np.exp(-0.5 * (x**2 + y**2 + z**2)) * np.cos(2 * np.sqrt(x**2 + y**2 + z**2))**2\n",
        "        plot_3d_pas_field_slice(q_theta_field, \"Qθ-Type Synchronizer\", output_plot_folder, '10_q_theta_field.png')\n",
        "\n",
        "        q_nu_field = np.exp(-0.5 * ((x-2)**2 + y**2 + z**2)) * np.cos(2 * np.sqrt((x+1)**2 + y**2))**2\n",
        "        plot_3d_pas_field_slice(q_nu_field, \"Qν-Type Entropy Sink\", output_plot_folder, '11_q_nu_field.png')\n",
        "\n",
        "        print(\"\\n🎉 Analysis complete! Check the 'analysis_plots' folder for your graphs.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "if __name__ == '__main__':\n",
        "    main()\n"
      ],

        "\n",
        "# --- Test compute_informational_current ---\n",
        "print(\"\\nTesting compute_informational_current...\")\n",
        "# Create a dummy 3D complex field A with some spatial variation\n",
        "grid_size = 32\n",
        "x, y, z = np.mgrid[:grid_size, :grid_size, :grid_size]\n",
        "# Example: a simple spiral phase and Gaussian magnitude\n",
        "magnitude = np.exp(-((x - grid_size//2)**2 + (y - grid_size//2)**2 + (z - grid_size//2)**2) / (grid_size/4)**2)\n",
        "phase = np.arctan2(y - grid_size//2, x - grid_size//2) + np.sin(z * 2 * np.pi / grid_size) * 2 # Add some z variation\n",
        "dummy_A_field = jnp.complex64(magnitude * np.exp(1j * phase))\n",
        "\n",
        "# Call compute_informational_current\n",
        "dummy_j_info = im.compute_informational_current(dummy_A_field)\n",
        "print(f\"Shape of computed J_info: {dummy_j_info.shape}\")\n",

            "Result shape for invalid input (wrong channels): (3, 0, 0, 0)\n",
            "Mapping current to B-field analogue...\n",
            "Error: Input J_info array has zero spatial dimensions.\n",
            "Result shape for invalid input (empty 4D): (3, 0, 0, 0)\n",
            "\n",
            "Testing visualize_magnetic_topologies...\n",
            "Saving visualization outputs to temporary directory: /tmp/tmp_flsk6e4\n",
            "Visualizing magnetic topologies and saving outputs to /tmp/tmp_flsk6e4...\n",
            "Saved basic magnitude slice visualization to /tmp/tmp_flsk6e4/b_field_magnitude_slice.png\n",

            "Visualizing magnetic topologies and saving outputs to /tmp/tmp_flsk6e4...\n",

            "B-field array has zero spatial dimensions, skipping visualization.\n",
            "\n",

        "import jax.numpy as jnp # Ensure jax.numpy is imported if not already\n",

        "    Visualizes the magnetic topologies of the B-field analogue.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",

        "\n",
        "    if not isinstance(b_field, jnp.ndarray) or b_field.ndim != 4 or b_field.shape[0] != 3:\n",

        "\n",

        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"

        "outputId": "f25f94d7-a348-4b4d-f28a-330ffebae1ee"

      "source": [
        "%%writefile -a informational_mapper.py\n",
        "\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp # Import jax.numpy for gradient calculation\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Core Informational Mapper Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def compute_informational_current(complex_field_A):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the J_info calculation based on the blueprint:\n",
        "    J_μ = κ * ρ * ∇_μ(ϕ)\n",
        "\n",
        "    We calculate the 3 spatial components (J_x, J_y, J_z).\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        complex_field_A (jnp.ndarray): The complex field A (e.g., 3D array).\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        jnp.ndarray: The informational current field (4D array: [3, grid, grid, grid]).\n",
        "                     Returns an empty array if input is invalid.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"Computing informational current...\")\n",
        "    if not isinstance(complex_field_A, jnp.ndarray) or complex_field_A.ndim != 3:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error: Expected 3D JAX array input, but got type {type(complex_field_A)} with {complex_field_A.ndim} dimensions.\")\n",
        "        return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0)) # Return empty array of appropriate shape on error\n",
        "\n",
        "    kappa = 1.0 # Assuming kappa = 1.0 as per previous code/blueprint\n",
        "\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # 1. Get rho (magnitude) and phi (phase)\n",
        "        # Per blueprint, J is proportional to rho, not rho^2\n",
        "        rho = jnp.abs(complex_field_A)\n",
        "        phi = jnp.angle(complex_field_A)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 2. Get gradient of phase: ∇(ϕ) = (dϕ/dx, dϕ/dy, dϕ/dz)\n",
        "        # jnp.gradient returns a tuple of arrays for a 3D input\n",
        "        grad_phi = jnp.gradient(phi)\n",
        "        grad_phi_x, grad_phi_y, grad_phi_z = grad_phi[0], grad_phi[1], grad_phi[2]\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 3. Calculate J = κ * ρ * ∇(ϕ)\n",
        "        J_x = kappa * rho * grad_phi_x\n",
        "        J_y = kappa * rho * grad_phi_y\n",
        "        J_z = kappa * rho * grad_phi_z\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Stack into a 4D array: (3, grid, grid, grid)\n",
        "        J_info_field = jnp.stack([J_x, J_y, J_z], axis=0)\n",
        "\n",
        "        print(\"Informational current computed.\")\n",
        "        return J_info_field\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error during informational current computation: {e}\")\n",
        "        return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0)) # Return empty array on error\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def map_current_to_B_field(j_info_field):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the B-field analogue mapping.\n",
        "\n",
        "    As specified, this is a direct mapping for visualization,\n",
        "    treating the spatial components of J_info as the B-field.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        j_info_field (jnp.ndarray): The informational current field (4D array: [3, grid, grid, grid]).\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        jnp.ndarray: The B-field analogue (4D array: [3, grid, grid, grid]).\n",
        "                     Returns an empty array if input is invalid.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"Mapping current to B-field analogue...\")\n",
        "    # J_info_field is already (3, G, G, G) as required for a 3-component vector field\n",
        "    # We can just return it, or save it as a separate file.\n",
        "    # For this implementation, we follow the \"direct mapping\" and return the input.\n",
        "\n",
        "    if not isinstance(j_info_field, jnp.ndarray) or j_info_field.ndim != 4 or j_info_field.shape[0] != 3:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error: Expected 4D JAX array with shape [3, ...], but got shape {j_info_field.shape}.\")\n",
        "        return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0)) # Return empty array on error\n",
        "\n",
        "    B_field_analogue = j_info_field\n",
        "\n",
        "    # In a real implementation, we might compute the curl: B = ∇ x J\n",
        "    # But for now, we follow the \"direct mapping\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    print(\"Current mapped to B-field analogue.\")\n",
        "    return B_field_analogue\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "# The visualize_magnetic_topologies function placeholder remains for now.\n",
        "# The example usage block remains a placeholder."
      ],
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Appending to informational_mapper.py\n"
          ]
        }
      ]

    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "d1855679",
        "outputId": "91c77a74-eac2-48f0-b0f3-635737134efc"
      },
      "source": [
        "%%writefile informational_mapper.py\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "This module implements the calculation and visualization of an informational\n",
        "B-field analogue based on the complex field A.\n",
        "\n",
        "It includes functions for:\n",
        "- Calculating the informational current (J_info).\n",
        "- Mapping J_info to a B-field analogue.\n",
        "- Visualizing the resulting magnetic topologies.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "import os\n",
        "import tempfile\n",
        "from typing import Dict, Any, Tuple, Union # Import necessary types\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Core Informational Mapper Functions ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def compute_informational_current(complex_field_A: jnp.ndarray, kappa: float = 1.0) -> jnp.ndarray:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Calculates the informational current (J_info) from the complex field A\n",
        "    using the formula J_μ = κ * ρ * ∇_μ(ϕ), where ρ is the magnitude of A\n",
        "    and ϕ is the phase of A.\n",
        "\n",
        "    The function computes the 3 spatial components of J_info (J_x, J_y, J_z).\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        complex_field_A (jnp.ndarray): The complex field A as a JAX 3D array\n",
        "                                       with shape (grid, grid, grid).\n",
        "        kappa (float): The scaling factor for the informational current. Defaults to 1.0.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        jnp.ndarray: The informational current field as a JAX 4D array\n",
        "                     with shape (3, grid, grid, grid), representing the vector\n",
        "                     field (J_x, J_y, J_z). Returns an empty array of shape (3, 0, 0, 0)\n",
        "                     if the input is invalid or an error occurs.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"Computing informational current...\")\n",
        "    if not isinstance(complex_field_A, jnp.ndarray) or complex_field_A.ndim != 3:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error: Expected 3D JAX array input, but got type {type(complex_field_A)} with {complex_field_A.ndim} dimensions.\")\n",
        "        return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0)) # Return empty array of appropriate shape on error\n",
        "    if complex_field_A.shape[0] == 0:\n",
        "         print(\"Error: Input 3D array is empty.\")\n",
        "         return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0))\n",
        "\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # 1. Get rho (magnitude) and phi (phase)\n",
        "        # Per blueprint, J is proportional to rho, not rho^2\n",
        "        rho: jnp.ndarray = jnp.abs(complex_field_A)\n",
        "        phi: jnp.ndarray = jnp.angle(complex_field_A)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 2. Get gradient of phase: ∇(ϕ) = (dϕ/dx, dϕ/dy, dϕ/dz)\n",
        "        # jnp.gradient returns a tuple of arrays for a 3D input\n",
        "        grad_phi: Tuple[jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray] = jnp.gradient(phi)\n",
        "        grad_phi_x, grad_phi_y, grad_phi_z = grad_phi[0], grad_phi[1], grad_phi[2]\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 3. Calculate J = κ * ρ * ∇(ϕ)\n",
        "        J_x: jnp.ndarray = kappa * rho * grad_phi_x\n",
        "        J_y: jnp.ndarray = kappa * rho * grad_phi_y\n",
        "        J_z: jnp.ndarray = kappa * rho * grad_phi_z\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Stack into a 4D array: (3, grid, grid, grid)\n",
        "        J_info_field: jnp.ndarray = jnp.stack([J_x, J_y, J_z], axis=0)\n",
        "\n",
        "        print(\"Informational current computed.\")\n",
        "        return J_info_field\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error during informational current computation: {e}\")\n",
        "        return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0)) # Return empty array on error\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def map_current_to_B_field(j_info_field: jnp.ndarray) -> jnp.ndarray:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the B-field analogue mapping as specified in the blueprint.\n",
        "\n",
        "    This is a direct mapping for visualization purposes, treating the spatial\n",
        "    components of the informational current (J_info) as the B-field.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        j_info_field (jnp.ndarray): The informational current field as a JAX 4D array\n",
        "                                    with shape (3, grid, grid, grid).\n",
        "\n",
        "    Returns:\n",
        "        jnp.ndarray: The B-field analogue as a JAX 4D array with shape\n",
        "                     (3, grid, grid, grid). Returns an empty array of shape\n",
        "                     (3, 0, 0, 0) if the input is invalid or an error occurs.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(\"Mapping current to B-field analogue...\")\n",
        "    # J_info_field is already (3, G, G, G) as required for a 3-component vector field\n",
        "    # We follow the \"direct mapping\" and return the input.\n",
        "\n",
        "    if not isinstance(j_info_field, jnp.ndarray) or j_info_field.ndim != 4 or j_info_field.shape[0] != 3:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error: Expected 4D JAX array with shape [3, ...], but got shape {j_info_field.shape}. Cannot map.\")\n",
        "        return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0)) # Return empty array on error\n",
        "    if j_info_field.shape[1] == 0:\n",
        "         print(\"Error: Input J_info array has zero spatial dimensions.\")\n",
        "         return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0))\n",
        "\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        B_field_analogue: jnp.ndarray = j_info_field\n",
        "\n",
        "        # In a more complex implementation, we might compute the curl: B = ∇ x J\n",
        "        # But for now, we follow the \"direct mapping\"\n",
        "\n",
        "        print(\"Current mapped to B-field analogue.\")\n",
        "        return B_field_analogue\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error during current to B-field mapping: {e}\")\n",
        "        return jnp.empty((3, 0, 0, 0)) # Return empty array on error\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "def visualize_magnetic_topologies(b_field: jnp.ndarray, output_path: str):\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Visualizes the magnetic topologies of the B-field analogue by saving\n",
        "    a central slice of the field magnitude as a heatmap image.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Args:\n",
        "        b_field (jnp.ndarray): The B-field analogue as a JAX 4D array\n",
        "                               with shape (3, grid, grid, grid).\n",
        "        output_path (str): The directory path to save the visualization output.\n",
        "                           A file named \"b_field_magnitude_slice.png\" will be created\n",
        "                           within this directory.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    print(f\"Visualizing magnetic topologies and saving outputs to {output_path}...\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    if not isinstance(b_field, jnp.ndarray) or b_field.ndim != 4 or b_field.shape[0] != 3:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error: Expected 4D JAX array with shape [3, ...], but got shape {b_field.shape}. Cannot visualize.\")\n",
        "        return\n",
        "    if b_field.shape[1] == 0:\n",
        "         print(\"B-field array has zero spatial dimensions, skipping visualization.\")\n",
        "         return\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Ensure the output directory exists\n",
        "    output_dir: str = output_path\n",
        "    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # --- Visualization Logic (Basic: Magnitude Slice) ---\n",
        "    # A real implementation could include stream plots, vector plots on slices, etc.\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Visualize the magnitude of the B-field on a central slice (e.g., Z-slice)\n",
        "        # Ensure the slice index is within bounds\n",
        "        slice_index_z: int = b_field.shape[3] // 2\n",
        "        if slice_index_z >= b_field.shape[3]:\n",
        "             slice_index_z = b_field.shape[3] - 1 # Adjust if grid size is small\n",
        "\n",
        "        b_magnitude_slice: jnp.ndarray = jnp.linalg.norm(b_field[:, :, :, slice_index_z], axis=0)\n",
        "\n",
        "        plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))\n",
        "        plt.imshow(np.asarray(b_magnitude_slice), cmap='viridis', origin='lower') # Convert JAX to NumPy for matplotlib\n",
        "        plt.title(\"Magnitude of B-field Analogue (Central Z-Slice)\")\n",
        "        plt.colorbar(label=\"Magnitude\")\n",
        "        plt.xlabel(\"X index\")\n",
        "        plt.ylabel(\"Y index\")\n",
        "        slice_output_path: str = os.path.join(output_dir, \"b_field_magnitude_slice.png\")\n",
        "        plt.savefig(slice_output_path)\n",
        "        plt.close()\n",
        "        print(f\"Saved basic magnitude slice visualization to {slice_output_path}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"Error during visualization: {e}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    print(\"Magnetic topology visualization complete.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
        "    print(\"Running informational_mapper.py as a script (example usage)...\")\n",
        "    import numpy as np\n",
        "    import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "    import os\n",
        "    import tempfile\n",
        "    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt # Ensure matplotlib is imported for visualization\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Create dummy complex field A\n",
        "    grid_size: int = 32\n",
        "    # Create a dummy field with some spatial variation\n",
        "    x, y, z = np.mgrid[:grid_size, :grid_size, :grid_size]\n",
        "    # Example: a simple spiral phase and Gaussian magnitude\n",
        "    magnitude = np.exp(-((x - grid_size//2)**2 + (y - grid_size//2)**2 + (z - grid_size//2)**2) / (grid_size/4)**2)\n",
        "    phase = np.arctan2(y - grid_size//2, x - grid_size//2) + np.sin(z * 2 * np.pi / grid_size) * 2 # Add some z variation\n",
        "    dummy_A_field: jnp.ndarray = jnp.complex64(magnitude * np.exp(1j * phase))\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Compute J_info\n",
        "    dummy_j_info: jnp.ndarray = compute_informational_current(dummy_A_field)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Map to B-field analogue\n",
        "    dummy_b_field: jnp.ndarray = map_current_to_B_field(dummy_j_info)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Visualize (using a temporary directory for output)\n",
        "    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp_dir:\n",
        "        print(f\"\\nSaving visualization outputs to temporary directory: {tmp_dir}\")\n",
        "        visualize_magnetic_topologies(dummy_b_field, tmp_dir) # Pass directory path\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Test with invalid input for compute_informational_current\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting compute_informational_current with invalid input...\")\n",
        "    invalid_input_2d: np.ndarray = np.random.rand(32, 32) # 2D numpy array\n",
        "    invalid_j_info = compute_informational_current(invalid_input_2d) # Should print error and return empty\n",
        "    print(f\"Result shape for invalid input: {invalid_j_info.shape}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Test with invalid input for map_current_to_B_field\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting map_current_to_B_field with invalid input...\")\n",
        "    invalid_input_3d: jnp.ndarray = jnp.ones((32, 32, 32)) # 3D JAX array (wrong shape)\n",
        "    invalid_b_field = map_current_to_B_field(invalid_input_3d) # Should print error and return empty\n",
        "    print(f\"Result shape for invalid input: {invalid_b_field.shape}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Test with invalid input for visualize_magnetic_topologies\n",
        "    print(\"\\nTesting visualize_magnetic_topologies with invalid input...\")\n",
        "    invalid_input_2d_b: jnp.ndarray = jnp.ones((3, 32, 32)) # 3D JAX array (wrong dimensions)\n",
        "    visualize_magnetic_topologies(invalid_input_2d_b, \".\") # Should print error\n",

        "\n",
        "# --- Test analyze_4d ---\n",
        "print(\"\\nTesting analyze_4d...\")\n",
        "# Create dummy 4D data and save to a temporary file\n",
        "dummy_rho_4d = np.random.rand(10, 32, 32, 32)\n",
        "with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=\".npy\", delete=False) as tmp_file_4d:\n",
        "    dummy_rho_4d_path = tmp_file_4d.name\n",
        "    np.save(dummy_rho_4d_path, dummy_rho_4d)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Run analyze_4d with the temporary file path\n",
        "results_4d = qm.analyze_4d(dummy_rho_4d_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"\\nResults from 4D analysis (analyzing last time step):\")\n",
        "print(f\"Total SSE: {results_4d.get('total_sse')}\")\n",
        "print(f\"Dominant Peak k: {results_4d.get('dominant_peak_k')}\")\n",
        "print(f\"Analyzed Time Step: {results_4d.get('analyzed_time_step')}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "if 'csv_files' in results_4d:\n",

        "    print(f\"\\nCleaned up temporary file: {dummy_rho_4d_path}\")\n",

              "      async function convertToInteractive(key) {\n",
              "        const element = document.querySelector('#df-bbb5adc8-45ad-4066-a2a1-cd4cbdc5af39');\n",
              "        const dataTable =\n",
              "          await google.colab.kernel.invokeFunction('convertToInteractive',\n",
              "                                                    [key], {});\n",
              "        if (!dataTable) return;\n",
              "\n",
              "        const docLinkHtml = 'Like what you see? Visit the ' +\n",
              "          '<a target=\"_blank\" href=https://colab.research.google.com/notebooks/data_table.ipynb>data table notebook</a>'\n",
              "          + ' to learn more about interactive tables.';\n",
              "        element.innerHTML = '';\n",
              "        dataTable['output_type'] = 'display_data';\n",
              "        await google.colab.output.renderOutput(dataTable, element);\n",
              "        const docLink = document.createElement('div');\n",
              "        docLink.innerHTML = docLinkHtml;\n",
              "        element.appendChild(docLink);\n",
              "      }\n",
              "    </script>\n",
              "  </div>\n",

              "        google.colab.kernel.accessAllowed ? 'block' : 'none';\n",
              "\n",
              "      async function convertToInteractive(key) {\n",
              "        const element = document.querySelector('#df-ae3cf251-4287-4b6e-8130-95718760ec0a');\n",
              "        const dataTable =\n",
              "          await google.colab.kernel.invokeFunction('convertToInteractive',\n",
              "                                                    [key], {});\n",
              "        if (!dataTable) return;\n",
              "\n",
              "        const docLinkHtml = 'Like what you see? Visit the ' +\n",
              "          '<a target=\"_blank\" href=https://colab.research.google.com/notebooks/data_table.ipynb>data table notebook</a>'\n",
              "          + ' to learn more about interactive tables.';\n",
              "        element.innerHTML = '';\n",
              "        dataTable['output_type'] = 'display_data';\n",
              "        await google.colab.output.renderOutput(dataTable, element);\n",
              "        const docLink = document.createElement('div');\n",
              "        docLink.innerHTML = docLinkHtml;\n",
              "        element.appendChild(docLink);\n",
              "      }\n",
              "    </script>\n",
              "  </div>\n",
              "\n",
              "\n",

              "        const docLink = document.createElement('div');\n",

              "      }\n",

              "type": "dataframe",
