            "# def map_current_to_B_field(J_info_field):\n",
            "#     \"\"\"\n",
            "#     Implements the B-field analogue mapping.\n",
            "# \n",
            "#     As specified, this is a direct mapping for visualization,\n",
            "#     treating the spatial components of J_info as the B-field.\n",
            "#     \"\"\"\n",
            "#     # J_info_field is already (3, G, G, G)\n",
            "#     # We can just return it, or save it as a separate file.\n",
            "#     # For this demo, we'll just return the input.\n",
            "#     B_field_analogue = J_info_field\n",
            "# \n",
            "#     # In a real implementation, we might compute the curl: B = ∇ x J\n",
            "#     # But for now, we follow the \"direct mapping\"\n",
            "# \n",
            "#     return B_field_analogue\n",
            "\n",
            "\"\"\"**Reasoning**:\n",
            "The `analysis_modules.py` file has been created. Now I can rerun the simulation code cell (`xYpafu6ro3Om`) which includes the import statement `import analysis_modules as am` and the calls to the analysis functions within the simulation loop. This should resolve the `ModuleNotFoundError` and the analysis functions should execute as intended.\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import tempfile\n",
            "import analysis_modules as am\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- 1. Simulation Setup ---\n",
            "GRID_SIZE = 64\n",
            "DT = 0.01\n",
            "STEPS = 1000 # Increase steps to allow for analysis intervals\n",
            "ANALYSIS_INTERVAL = 100 # Run analysis every 100 steps\n",
            "\n",
            "# Define parameters based on the S-NCGL blueprints\n",
            "# These values are illustrative\n",
            "sim_params = {\n",
            "    'epsilon': 0.1,         # Linear growth\n",
            "    'D': 1.0,               # Diffusion coefficient (real part of ∇²)\n",
            "    'c1': 0.5,              # Linear dispersion (imaginary part of ∇²)\n",
            "    'b3': 1.0,              # Nonlinear saturation (real part of |A|²A)\n",
            "    'c3': -1.5,             # Nonlinear phase shift (imag. part of |A|²A)\n",
            "    'threshold': 1.2,       # Collapse threshold for rho = |A|²\n",
            "    'splash_fraction': 0.5, # Fraction of A to redistribute\n",
            "    'splash_kernel_sigma': 4.0, # Width of Gaussian splash\n",
            "    'reset_value': 0.0 + 0.0j,  # Value to reset collapsed cells to\n",
            "    'source': 0.0 + 0.0j    # Static source term (can be a 3D array)\n",
            "}\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- 2. Pre-compute Kernels ---\n",
            "print(\"Pre-computing kernels...\")\n",
            "k_squared, K_fft = precompute_kernels(\n",
            "    GRID_SIZE,\n",
            "    sim_params['splash_kernel_sigma']\n",
            ")\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- 3. Initialize State ---\n",
            "print(\"Initializing complex field A...\")\n",
            "key = jax.random.PRNGKey(42)\n",
            "key_real, key_imag = jax.random.split(key)\n",
            "\n",
            "shape = (GRID_SIZE, GRID_SIZE, GRID_SIZE)\n",
            "\n",
            "# Initialize with small random complex values\n",
            "A_field_initial = (\n",
            "    jax.random.normal(key_real, shape) * 0.1 +\n",
            "    1j * jax.random.normal(key_imag, shape) * 0.1\n",
            ")\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- 4. JIT Compilation (Warm-up) ---\n",
            "print(\"Compiling JAX function...\")\n",
            "start_compile = time.time()\n",
            "\n",
            "# Run the function once to compile it\n",
            "A_field_compiled = s_ncgl_full_step(\n",
            "    A_field_initial,\n",
            "    sim_params,\n",
            "    k_squared,\n",
            "    K_fft,\n",
            "    GRID_SIZE,\n",
            "    DT\n",
            ").block_until_ready()\n",
            "\n",
            "end_compile = time.time()\n",
            "print(f\"Compilation finished in {end_compile - start_compile:.4f} seconds.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- 5. Run Simulation ---\n",
            "print(f\"Running simulation for {STEPS} steps with analysis every {ANALYSIS_INTERVAL} steps...\")\n",
            "A_field_current = A_field_compiled\n",
            "\n",
            "start_run = time.time()\n",
            "\n",
            "# Use a JAX loop for performance (optional, but good practice)\n",
            "# def simulation_loop(i, A):\n",
            "#     return s_ncgl_full_step(A, sim_params, k_squared, K_fft, GRID_SIZE, DT)\n",
            "\n",
            "# We can run the loop in Python\n",
            "for i in range(STEPS):\n",
            "    A_field_current = s_ncgl_full_step(\n",
            "        A_field_current,\n",
            "        sim_params,\n",
            "        k_squared,\n",
            "        K_fft,\n",
            "        GRID_SIZE,\n",
            "        DT\n",
            "    )\n",
            "\n",
            "    # Run analysis at specified intervals\n",
            "    if (i + 1) % ANALYSIS_INTERVAL == 0 or (i + 1) == STEPS:\n",
            "        print(f\"\\n--- Running analysis after step {i + 1} ---\")\n",
            "\n",
            "        # Save the current state to a temporary file\n",
            "        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=\".npy\", delete=False) as tmp_file:\n",
            "            temp_file_path = tmp_file.name\n",
            "            jnp.save(temp_file_path, A_field_current)\n",
            "\n",
            "        try:\n",
            "            # Run Quantule Mapper analysis\n",
            "            print(\"Running Quantule Mapper...\")\n",
            "            quantule_results = am.run_quantule_mapper(temp_file_path)\n",
            "            print(f\"  Quantule Mapper Total SSE: {quantule_results.get('total_sse'):.4f}\")\n",
            "            print(f\"  Quantule Mapper Dominant Peak k: {quantule_results.get('dominant_peak_k'):.4f}\")\n",
            "            if 'csv_files' in quantule_results:\n",
            "                for csv_name, df in quantule_results['csv_files'].items():\n",
            "                    print(f\"  Quantule Mapper generated {csv_name}\")\n",
            "                    # Optionally display or save these dataframes\n",
            "\n",
            "            # Run Informational Current analysis\n",
            "            print(\"Running Informational Current analysis...\")\n",
            "            j_info_field = am.compute_informational_current(temp_file_path)\n",
            "            j_info_mean_magnitude = jnp.mean(jnp.linalg.norm(j_info_field, axis=0))\n",
            "            print(f\"  Mean magnitude of J_info: {j_info_mean_magnitude:.4f}\")\n",
            "            # In a real scenario, you might save j_info_field or map_current_to_B_field\n",
            "\n",
            "        except Exception as e:\n",
            "            print(f\"Analysis failed at step {i + 1}: {e}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "        finally:\n",
            "            # Clean up the temporary file\n",
            "            if os.path.exists(temp_file_path):\n",
            "                os.remove(temp_file_path)\n",
            "                # print(f\"Removed temporary file: {temp_file_path}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# Block to ensure all computation is finished before stopping timer\n",
            "A_field_current.block_until_ready()\n",
            "end_run = time.time()\n",
            "\n",
            "print(f\"\\nTotal simulation run time: {end_run - start_run:.4f} seconds.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- 6. Final State Analysis ---\n",
            "final_rho = jnp.abs(A_field_current)**2\n",
            "print(f\"\\nFinal State Summary:\")\n",
            "print(f\"  Max Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.max(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",
            "print(f\"  Min Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.min(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",
            "print(f\"  Mean Rho (Resonance Density): {jnp.mean(final_rho):.4f}\")\n",

            "GRID_SIZE = 64\n",

            "    'threshold': 1.2,       # Collapse threshold for rho = |A|²\n",

            "}\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- 2. Pre-compute Kernels ---\n",
            "print(\"Pre-computing kernels...\")\n",
            "k_squared, K_fft = precompute_kernels(\n",
            "    GRID_SIZE,\n",
            "    sim_params['splash_kernel_sigma']\n",
            ")\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- 3. Initialize State ---\n",
            "print(\"Initializing complex field A...\")\n",
            "key = jax.random.PRNGKey(42)\n",

            "\n",
            "shape = (GRID_SIZE, GRID_SIZE, GRID_SIZE)\n",
            "\n",
            "# Initialize with small random complex values\n",

            "    jax.random.normal(key_real, shape) * 0.1 +\n",
            "    1j * jax.random.normal(key_imag, shape) * 0.1\n",
            ")\n",

            "#     return s_ncgl_full_step(A, sim_params, k_squared, K_fft, GRID_SIZE, DT)\n",

            "        K_fft,\n",
            "        GRID_SIZE,\n",
            "        DT\n",
            "    )\n",
            "\n",
            "    # Run analysis at specified intervals\n",
            "    if (i + 1) % ANALYSIS_INTERVAL == 0 or (i + 1) == STEPS:\n",
            "        current_step = i + 1\n",
            "        print(f\"\\n--- Running analysis after step {current_step} ---\")\n",
            "\n",
            "        # Save the current state to a temporary file\n",
            "        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=\".npy\", delete=False) as tmp_file:\n",
            "            temp_file_path = tmp_file.name\n",

            "            print(f\"  Quantule Mapper Dominant Peak k: {quantule_results.get('dominant_peak_k'):.4f}\")\n",
            "\n",

            "                for csv_name, df in quantule_results['csv_files'].items():\n",
            "                    output_filename = f\"{os.path.splitext(csv_name)[0]}_step_{current_step}.csv\"\n",
            "                    output_path = os.path.join(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR, output_filename)\n",
            "                    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)\n",
            "                    print(f\"  Saved {output_filename}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "            # Run Informational Current analysis\n",
            "            print(\"Running Informational Current analysis...\")\n",
            "            j_info_field = am.compute_informational_current(temp_file_path)\n",
            "            j_info_mean_magnitude = jnp.mean(jnp.linalg.norm(j_info_field, axis=0))\n",
            "            print(f\"  Mean magnitude of J_info: {j_info_mean_magnitude:.4f}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "            # Save the full J_info_field (B-field analogue)\n",
            "            j_info_filename = f\"j_info_field_step_{current_step}.npy\"\n",
            "            j_info_path = os.path.join(ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR, j_info_filename)\n",
            "            jnp.save(j_info_path, j_info_field)\n",
            "            print(f\"  Saved {j_info_filename}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        except Exception as e:\n",
            "            print(f\"Analysis failed at step {current_step}: {e}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "        finally:\n",
            "            # Clean up the temporary file\n",
            "            if os.path.exists(temp_file_path):\n",
            "                os.remove(temp_file_path)\n",
            "                # print(f\"Removed temporary file: {temp_file_path}\")\n",
            "\n",

            "\n",
            "*   The quantule mapper logic, including multi-ray FFT, log-prime SSE calculation, and quantule classification, was successfully added as a dedicated code module.\n",

            "*   The simulation loop was modified to call the analysis functions at specified intervals (every 100 steps in this case) by saving the current simulation state to a temporary file and passing its path to the analysis functions.\n",
            "*   Analysis results (Quantule Mapper SSE, dominant peak k, and Mean magnitude of Informational Current) were printed during the simulation run.\n",
            "*   The outputs from the analysis modules, specifically the Quantule Mapper CSVs and the Informational Current field (`j_info_field` as a .npy array), were successfully saved to a dedicated output directory (`simulation_analysis_outputs`) at each analysis interval.\n",
            "*   Temporary files created for analysis were correctly cleaned up after each analysis step.\n",
            "*   Initial simulation analysis showed an \"inf\" SSE and 0.0 dominant peak k from the Quantule Mapper, suggesting early simulation states may not exhibit structures easily mappable to log-prime targets with current analysis parameters.\n",
            "*   The mean magnitude of the Informational Current showed a decreasing trend over the simulation steps.\n",
            "\n",
            "### Insights or Next Steps\n",
            "\n",
            "*   Investigate the Quantule Mapper parameters (e.g., peak finding prominence/distance) or analyze later simulation states to see if more structured patterns emerge that align with log-prime targets.\n",
            "*   Develop visualization scripts to load and inspect the saved Quantule Mapper CSVs and the Informational Current (.npy) files to gain deeper insights into the spatial distribution and evolution of these analysis outputs.\n",
            "\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "This module provides a toolkit for ensuring scientific reproducibility in\n",
            "simulation runs, extracted from the IRER project's adaptive hunt scripts.\n",
            "\n",
            "It includes functions for:\n",
            "1.  File Fingerprinting (SHA256)\n",
            "2.  Run Manifest Generation (Hashing all outputs and params)\n",
            "3.  Hash-Based Feedback Seed Generation (for deterministic adaptive loops)\n",
            "4.  CSV Ledger Logging\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "\n",
            "import numpy as np\n",
            "import pandas as pd\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import hashlib\n",
            "import pathlib\n",
            "from typing import Dict, Any\n",
            "\n",
            "def save_fingerprint(file_path: str, verbose: bool = False):\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Calculates the SHA256 hash of a single file and saves it to a\n",
            "    corresponding .sha256sum file.\n",
            "\n",
            "    This is a core component for verifying data integrity.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        path_obj = pathlib.Path(file_path)\n",
            "        if path_obj.is_file():\n",
            "            file_hash = hashlib.sha256()\n",
            "            with open(path_obj, 'rb') as f:\n",
            "                # Read in chunks for large files\n",
            "                while chunk := f.read(8192):\n",
            "                    file_hash.update(chunk)\n",
            "\n",
            "            fingerprint = file_hash.hexdigest()\n",
            "            sha256_file_path = path_obj.with_suffix(path_obj.suffix + \".sha256sum\")\n",
            "\n",
            "            with open(sha256_file_path, 'w', newline='\\n') as f_sha:\n",
            "                f_sha.write(fingerprint + f\" *{path_obj.name}\\n\")\n",
            "\n",
            "            if verbose:\n",
            "                print(f\"  SHA256: {path_obj.name} -> {sha256_file_path.name}\")\n",
            "        elif verbose:\n",
            "            print(f\"  Warning (fingerprint): File not found: {file_path}\")\n",
            "    except Exception as e:\n",
            "        print(f\"  Error creating fingerprint for {file_path}: {e}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "def generate_run_manifest_fingerprint(run_output_dir: str,\n",
            "                                      params_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n",
            "                                      verbose: bool = False) -> str:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Generates a master manifest file for a simulation run.\n",
            "\n",
            "    This file includes:\n",
            "    - The simulation parameters used.\n",
            "    - SHA256 hashes of all key (non-binary) output files.\n",
            "    - An overall manifest hash for the entire run's provenance.\n",
            "\n",
            "    This is the primary function for run logging and reproducibility.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    overall_manifest_hash_value = \"GENERATION_FAILED\"\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        run_dir_path = pathlib.Path(run_output_dir)\n",
            "        if not run_dir_path.is_dir():\n",
            "            if verbose: print(f\"  Warning (manifest): Directory not found: {run_output_dir}\");\n",
            "            return overall_manifest_hash_value\n",
            "\n",
            "        file_manifest_details = []\n",
            "        # Recursively find all files in the run directory\n",
            "        for p in sorted(run_dir_path.rglob(\"*\")):\n",
            "            # Exclude large binaries (like .npy) and other manifests\n",
            "            if p.is_file() and not p.name.endswith((\".sha256sum\", \".npz\", \".npy\")) and p.name != \"run_manifest.sha256sum\":\n",
            "                try:\n",
            "                    file_hash = hashlib.sha256()\n",
            "                    with open(p, 'rb') as f_p:\n",
            "                        while chunk := f_p.read(8192):\n",
            "                            file_hash.update(chunk)\n",
            "                    relative_path_str = str(p.relative_to(run_dir_path)).replace(\"\\\\\", \"/\")\n",
            "                    file_manifest_details.append(f\"{file_hash.hexdigest()} *{relative_path_str}\")\n",
            "                except Exception as e_file:\n",
            "                    if verbose: print(f\"  Error hashing file {p} for manifest: {e_file}\")\n",
            "                    relative_path_str = str(p.relative_to(run_dir_path)).replace(\"\\\\\", \"/\")\n",
            "                    file_manifest_details.append(f\"ERROR_HASHING *{relative_path_str}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "        manifest_string = \"\\n\".join(sorted(file_manifest_details))\n",
            "\n",
            "        # --- Serialize parameters for the manifest ---\n",
            "        # Ensure NumPy types are converted to standard Python types for JSON\n",
            "        serializable_params = {}\n",
            "        if params_dict:\n",
            "            for k, v in params_dict.items():\n",
            "                if isinstance(v, np.generic):\n",
            "                    serializable_params[k] = v.item()\n",
            "                else:\n",
            "                    serializable_params[k] = v\n",
            "\n",
            "        provenance_content = \"--- Parameters ---\\n\"\n",
            "        provenance_content += json.dumps(serializable_params, sort_keys=True, indent=2) + \"\\n\"\n",
            "\n",
            "        # --- Combine all provenance info for a final hash ---\n",
            "        full_manifest_content_for_hashing = provenance_content + \"--- File Hashes (selected) ---\\n\" + manifest_string\n",
            "        overall_manifest_hash_value = hashlib.sha256(full_manifest_content_for_hashing.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()\n",
            "\n",
            "        # --- Write the manifest file ---\n",
            "        manifest_file_path = run_dir_path / \"run_manifest.sha256sum\"\n",
            "        with open(manifest_file_path, 'w', newline='\\n') as f_manifest:\n",
            "            f_manifest.write(f\"Overall Run Manifest Hash (SHA256 of below content sections): {overall_manifest_hash_value}\\n\\n\")\n",
            "            if params_dict:\n",
            "                f_manifest.write(\"--- Parameters ---\\n\")\n",
            "                json.dump(serializable_params, f_manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True)\n",
            "                f_manifest.write(\"\\n\\n\")\n",
            "            f_manifest.write(\"--- Individual File SHA256 Hashes (*relative_path_to_file, selected files) ---\\n\")\n",
            "            f_manifest.write(manifest_string + \"\\n\")\n",
            "\n",
            "        if verbose: print(f\"  Run manifest for '{run_output_dir}' saved to {manifest_file_path.name}\")\n",
            "        return overall_manifest_hash_value\n",
            "    except Exception as e:\n",
            "        print(f\"  Error creating run manifest for {run_output_dir}: {e}\")\n",
            "        return overall_manifest_hash_value\n",
            "\n",
            "def compute_feedback_seed_adaptive(summary_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> int:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Computes the next seed for an adaptive loop based on the summary\n",
            "    of the previous run. This creates a deterministic, hash-based chain\n",
            "    for reproducible adaptive searches.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "\n",
            "    # Extract key metrics that define the run's outcome\n",
            "    key_metrics = {\n",
            "        'total_collapses': summary_dict.get('total_collapses'),\n",
            "        'avg_rho_final': summary_dict.get('avg_rho_final'),\n",
            "        'max_rho_overall': summary_dict.get('max_rho_overall'),\n",
            "        'k_peak_value': summary_dict.get('k_peak_value'),\n",
            "        'k_peak_met_target': summary_dict.get('k_peak_met_target'),\n",
            "        'is_coherent': summary_dict.get('is_coherent'),\n",
            "    }\n",
            "\n",
            "    evo_round = summary_dict.get('simulation_parameters', {}).get('evolution_round', -1)\n",
            "    manifest_hash = summary_dict.get('overall_manifest_hash', \"\")\n",
            "\n",
            "    if not manifest_hash or manifest_hash == \"GENERATION_FAILED\":\n",
            "        print(\"Warning: Manifest hash not available for feedback seed. Seed will be less robustly deterministic.\")\n",
            "        # Fallback to hashing parameters if manifest failed\n",
            "        params_for_hash = summary_dict.get('simulation_parameters', {})\n",
            "        serializable_params = {k: (v.item() if isinstance(v, np.generic) else v) for k, v in params_for_hash.items()}\n",
            "        manifest_hash = hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(serializable_params, sort_keys=True).encode()).hexdigest()\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- Serialize all metrics for hashing ---\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        serializable_key_metrics = {}\n",
            "        for k, v in key_metrics.items():\n",
            "            if isinstance(v, (np.bool_, bool)):\n",
            "                serializable_key_metrics[k] = bool(v)\n",
            "            elif isinstance(v, (np.integer, int)):\n",
            "                serializable_key_metrics[k] = int(v)\n",
            "            elif isinstance(v, (np.floating, float)):\n",
            "                serializable_key_metrics[k] = float(v) if np.isfinite(v) else None\n",
            "            elif v is None:\n",
            "                 serializable_key_metrics[k] = None\n",
            "            else:\n",
            "                serializable_key_metrics[k] = str(v)\n",
            "\n",
            "        combined_data_str = (\n",
            "            json.dumps(serializable_key_metrics, sort_keys=True) +\n",
            "            f\"evo_round:{evo_round}\" +\n",
            "            str(manifest_hash)\n",
            "        )\n",
            "    except TypeError as e_json:\n",
            "        print(f\"Error serializing key_metrics for feedback seed: {e_json}\")\n",
            "        combined_data_str = str(key_metrics) + f\"evo_round:{evo_round}\" + str(manifest_hash)\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- Generate the new seed ---\n",
            "    new_seed_hex = hashlib.sha256(combined_data_str.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()\n",
            "    # Use top 8 hex chars (32 bits) for the new seed\n",
            "    new_seed = int(new_seed_hex[:8], 16)\n",
            "    return new_seed\n",
            "\n",
            "def log_run_to_csv_ledger(ledger_path: str, summary_dict: Dict[str, Any]):\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Appends a single run's summary and parameters to a CSV ledger file.\n",
            "    This function is designed to be called after each simulation run.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        # Flatten the nested parameter dictionary for clean CSV columns\n",
            "        ledger_entry = {}\n",
            "        sim_params = summary_dict.get('simulation_parameters', {})\n",
            "\n",
            "        # Add summary metrics\n",
            "        ledger_entry['evolution_round'] = sim_params.get('evolution_round')\n",
            "        ledger_entry['global_seed'] = sim_params.get('global_seed')\n",
            "        ledger_entry['run_name'] = sim_params.get('run_name_suffix')\n",
            "        ledger_entry['manifest_hash'] = summary_dict.get('overall_manifest_hash')\n",
            "        ledger_entry['total_collapses'] = summary_dict.get('total_collapses')\n",
            "        ledger_entry['avg_rho_final'] = summary_dict.get('avg_rho_final')\n",
            "        ledger_entry['max_rho_overall'] = summary_dict.get('max_rho_overall')\n",
            "\n",
            "        # Add analysis metrics if they exist\n",
            "        if 'k_peak_value' in summary_dict:\n",
            "            ledger_entry['k_peak_value'] = summary_dict.get('k_peak_value')\n",
            "            ledger_entry['k_peak_met_target'] = summary_dict.get('k_peak_met_target')\n",
            "            ledger_entry['is_coherent'] = summary_dict.get('is_coherent')\n",
            "\n",
            "        # Add all simulation parameters, prefixed with 'param_'\n",
            "        for p_key, p_val in sim_params.items():\n",
            "            ledger_entry[f\"param_{p_key}\"] = p_val\n",
            "\n",
            "        # Convert to DataFrame to handle CSV operations robustly\n",
            "        df_entry = pd.DataFrame([ledger_entry])\n",
            "\n",
            "        # Check if file exists to write header or append\n",
            "        file_exists = os.path.isfile(ledger_path)\n",
            "\n",
            "        df_entry.to_csv(ledger_path, mode='a', header=not file_exists, index=False)\n",
            "\n",
            "    except Exception as e:\n",
            "        print(f\"Error updating CSV ledger at {ledger_path}: {e}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\"\"\"# Task\n",
            "Integrate the provenance functions from the code cell `OFqR4VzHtfLJ` into the simulation loop in cell `xYpafu6ro3Om`.\n",
            "\n",
            "## Save provenance module to file\n",
            "\n",
            "### Subtask:\n",
            "Save the code from the provenance module cell (`OFqR4VzHtfLJ`) to a Python file named `provenance_tools.py`.\n",
            "\n",
            "**Reasoning**:\n",
            "To make the provenance functions available for import, I need to save the code from the provided cell (`OFqR4VzHtfLJ`) into a Python file. I will use the `%%writefile` magic command to create `provenance_tools.py` and write the code into it.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "\n",
            "# Commented out IPython magic to ensure Python compatibility.\n",
            "# %%writefile provenance_tools.py\n",

            "#         print(f\"  Error creating run manifest for {run_output_dir}: {e}\")\n",
            "#         return overall_manifest_hash_value\n",
            "# \n",
            "# def compute_feedback_seed_adaptive(summary_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> int:\n",
